question: PRC stands for?
keyMemory: Kapag PRC, ang automatic memory mo dapat ay: Professional Regulation Commission. Huwag malito sa Professional Regulatory Board, kasi PRB iyon, hindi PRC.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. Professional Regulation Commission.
Ulitin natin: PRC means Professional Regulation Commission.
Ito ang tamang sagot kasi ang official name ng PRC ay Professional Regulation Commission. Sa Philippine professional practice, lalo na sa ALE, kailangan kabisado mo ito dahil PRC ang government agency na nagre-regulate ng licensure exams, registration, licensing, at supervision ng regulated professions, kasama na ang architecture. Official PRC history describes PRC as the licensing and regulatory agency of the national government for regulated professions. (Professional Regulation Commission)
Ngayon, ang common trap dito ay yung word na Regulatory. Kasi sa RA 9266, makikita mo rin ang term na Professional Regulatory Board of Architecture. So may “Regulatory” talaga sa ibang context. Pero tandaan mo:
PRB is Professional Regulatory Board.
PRC is Professional Regulation Commission.
Kaya kapag nakita mo ang PRC, huwag kang mahila ng word na “Regulatory.” Ang tamang expansion ay Professional Regulation Commission.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
Choice A, Philippines Regulation Commission, mali kasi hindi “Philippines” ang first word. Hindi ito commission of the Philippines by name. Ang focus ng name ay professionals, kaya Professional.
Choice B, Philippines Regulatory Commission, mali rin. Dalawang problema: “Philippines” ang ginamit, at “Regulatory” pa. Hindi ito official name.
Choice C, Professional Regulatory Commission, ito ang pinaka-trap. Mukhang tama kasi “professional” at “regulatory” sound very legal. Pero sa official name, hindi “Regulatory Commission.” Ang official ay Regulation Commission.
Choice D, Professional Regulation Commission, exact official name. Kaya ito ang sagot.
Elimination method natin dito:
Una, tanggalin agad lahat ng nagsisimula sa Philippines. So tanggal A and B. Ang PRC ay hindi “Philippines,” kundi Professional.
Natira C and D. Dito ang laban ay Regulatory versus Regulation. Tandaan mo: PRC equals Regulation Commission. PRB equals Regulatory Board. Kaya tanggal C, piliin D.
Ito ang iniisip mo dapat habang nagsasagot:
“PRC agency ito. Official name ba ito o description lang? Ah, official name: Professional Regulation Commission.”
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag PRC, ang automatic memory mo dapat ay: Professional Regulation Commission. Huwag malito sa Professional Regulatory Board, kasi PRB iyon, hindi PRC.

question: What is the law that created the Professional Regulation Commission or PRC?
keyMemory: Kapag tinanong “What law created the PRC?”, ang sagot ay PD 223. Kapag tinanong naman modernization of PRC, doon papasok ang RA 8981.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. PD 223.
Ulitin natin: The law that created the PRC is PD 223, or Presidential Decree No. 223.
Ito ang tamang sagot kasi ang Professional Regulation Commission was first created as a national government agency by Presidential Decree No. 223, dated June 22, 1973. PRC’s own official history states this, and the title of PD 223 is about creating the Professional Regulation Commission and prescribing its powers and functions. (Professional Regulation Commission)
Board exam logic ito: kapag tinanong, created the PRC, ang sagot ay PD 223.
May possible confusion dito: RA 8981 is the PRC Modernization Act of 2000. Ibig sabihin, minodernize niya ang PRC later on. Pero kung ang tanong ay created, original creation ang hinahanap. Kaya PD 223, hindi RA 8981. Sa choices naman wala ang RA 8981, pero useful ito para hindi ka malito kapag lumabas sa ibang question.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
Choice A, RA 232, mali. Una, Republic Act siya, pero ang creation of PRC ay under Presidential Decree, hindi Republic Act. Pangalawa, mali rin ang number.
Choice B, PD 223, tama. Same law type, same number. Presidential Decree No. 223.
Choice C, PD 232, trap ito. Presidential Decree nga siya, so medyo mukhang plausible. Pero mali ang number. Ang kailangan mong tandaan ay 223, hindi 232. Parang binaliktad lang yung digits para mahuli ka.
Choice D, RA 223, trap din. Tama ang number na 223, pero mali ang law type. Hindi siya RA. PD siya. So hindi sapat na 223 lang ang maalala mo. Dapat buong label: PD 223.
Elimination method:
Una, hanapin mo muna kung RA ba o PD. Since PRC was created during the Marcos decree period in 1973, ang clue mo ay PD, Presidential Decree. So tanggal A and D kasi RA sila.
Natira B and C: PD 223 versus PD 232. Dito number memory ang kailangan. Ang exact law is PD 223. Kaya tanggal C, piliin B.
Ito ang reason bakit natitira ang B:
Siya lang ang may tamang combination: PD + 223.
Common trap sa board exam:
They will separate the correct number from the correct law type. For example, RA 223 may look familiar because of “223,” but wrong siya kasi hindi RA. PD 232 may look legal because of “PD,” but wrong siya kasi mali ang number.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag tinanong “What law created the PRC?”, ang sagot ay PD 223. Kapag tinanong naman modernization of PRC, doon papasok ang RA 8981.

question: RA 9266, known as “An Act to Regulate the Practice of Architecture in the Philippines,” was signed and sealed on?
keyMemory: RA 9266 signed: March 17, 2004. RA 9266 effective: April 10, 2004.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. March 17, 2004.
Ulitin natin: RA 9266 was signed on March 17, 2004.
Ang keyword dito ay “signed”. Kapag signed, ang hinahanap ay date kung kailan na-approve o napirmahan ang batas. Hindi siya effectivity date.
Important distinction ito sa ALE:
March 17, 2004 — signed or approved.
April 10, 2004 — naging effective.
Kaya kapag nakita mo ang signed, huwag kang pupunta sa April 10. Trap iyon. Ang April 10 ay tama lang kung ang tanong ay “became effective”.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
b. April 10, 2004 — mali dito kasi effectivity date ito, hindi signing date.
c. Nov. 14, 2004 — hindi ito date of signing.
d. Dec. 1, 2004 — related ito sa later effectivity/publication context ng IRR, hindi sa signing ng RA 9266 mismo.
Elimination method:
Basahin mo muna ang keyword: signed. Kapag signed, hanapin mo earliest official approval date. Tanggal agad ang April 10 kung alam mong effectivity iyon. Tanggal din ang November at December kasi too late na for signing ng law. Naiiwan ang March 17, 2004.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
RA 9266 signed: March 17, 2004.
RA 9266 effective: April 10, 2004.

question: What is the difference between RA 545 and RA 9266 in terms of architectural practice?
keyMemory: Kapag RA 545 versus RA 9266 at ang usapan ay scope of practice, tandaan mo: structural conceptualization.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Structural conceptualization.
Ulitin natin: The difference in terms of architectural practice is structural conceptualization.
Ito ang tamang sagot kasi sa RA 9266, sa definition ng practice of architecture, pumasok ang phrase na architectural and structural conceptualization. Ibig sabihin, kasama sa general practice of architecture ang pag-iisip at pagbuo ng basic structural concept in relation to the architectural design.
Pero malinaw dapat: hindi ibig sabihin nito na architect ang gagawa ng full structural engineering computation. Ang ibig sabihin ng structural conceptualization ay conceptual level: choosing, arranging, and proportioning the structural system as part of the building concept. Sa actual detailed engineering design, papasok pa rin ang structural engineer.
Bakit mali ang iba?
a. Two-years diversified experience — this is more about qualification or experience requirement, hindi siya main difference sa definition of architectural practice.
c. Corporate practice — important siya sa RA 9266, pero ang tanong ay in terms of architectural practice, kaya ang hinahanap ay nasa scope or definition ng practice.
d. Authorship of documents — important din sa RA 9266, pero this is more about ownership and rights over documents, hindi siya yung practice-scope difference na tinatanong.
Elimination method:
Ang clue ay “in terms of architectural practice.” So hanapin mo yung choice na may kinalaman sa scope of services ng architect.
Two-years diversified experience? Qualification. Tanggal.
Corporate practice? Registration setup. Tanggal.
Authorship? Documents and ownership. Tanggal.
Structural conceptualization? Scope of architectural practice. Iyon ang sagot.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag RA 545 versus RA 9266 at ang usapan ay scope of practice, tandaan mo: structural conceptualization.

question: What is now allowed by RA 9266 which was not allowed in RA 545?
keyMemory: RA 545: corporations cannot register. RA 9266: corporate practice allowed, but only under strict architect-controlled conditions.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Corporate practice.
Ulitin natin: RA 9266 allowed corporate practice, subject to requirements.
Dito ang tanong ay hindi na “scope of practice.” Ang tanong ay “what is now allowed”. Ang clue ay yung word na allowed. Under RA 545, corporations could not register for architectural practice. Under RA 9266, allowed na ang firm, partnership, corporation, or association to practice architecture, pero may strict conditions.
Hindi ito open to any corporation. Hindi pwedeng basta construction company or business group lang. Sa RA 9266, dapat controlled ito by properly registered and licensed architects, and at least 75% of the owners, shareholders, incorporators, directors, or executive officers must be registered and licensed architects.
Bakit mali ang iba?
a. Two-years diversified experience — hindi ito “now allowed.” Requirement ito for registration/exam context.
b. Structural conceptualization — ito yung difference in scope of practice, pero hindi exactly “allowed corporate registration.”
d. Authorship of documents — this concerns intellectual property and ownership of plans/specifications, hindi siya corporate practice.
Elimination method:
Keyword: “now allowed”. Ibig sabihin may dating bawal, tapos naging pwede under RA 9266.
Dating bawal ang corporation na mag-register sa architecture practice under RA 545. RA 9266 made it possible. Kaya corporate practice ang sagot.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
RA 545: corporations cannot register.
RA 9266: corporate practice allowed, but only under strict architect-controlled conditions.

question: What do you call Working Drawings, Specifications, Estimates and General Conditions?
keyMemory: Kapag drawings, specifications, conditions, and other agreement documents are grouped together, ang term ay Contract Documents.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Contract Documents.
Ulitin natin: Working Drawings, Specifications, Estimates, and General Conditions are Contract Documents.
Ito ang tamang sagot kasi kapag pinagsama-sama mo ang drawings, specifications, conditions, estimates, and other written documents used for the agreement and implementation of the project, ang tawag diyan ay contract documents.
Ang working drawings alone are not the whole contract package. The specifications explain materials and workmanship. The general conditions explain rights, responsibilities, procedures, and obligations. Kapag sila ay part ng construction agreement, they form the contract documents.
Bakit mali ang iba?
a. Construction Drawings — too narrow. Drawings lang ito. Hindi kasama ang specifications and general conditions.
c. Project Manual — possible na may specifications and bidding/contract information inside, pero hindi ito ang best answer dito. The question asks the collective legal/contract set.
d. Project Requirements — vague and too broad. Hindi ito standard answer sa professional practice context.
Elimination method:
Look at the list: drawings plus specs plus general conditions. Kapag marami itong project documents na attached to the agreement, hindi na siya simpleng drawing set. Legal package na siya. Kaya tanggal Construction Drawings kasi drawings lang. Tanggal Project Requirements kasi vague. Between Project Manual and Contract Documents, mas complete and legal ang Contract Documents.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag drawings, specifications, conditions, and other agreement documents are grouped together, ang term ay Contract Documents.
________________________________________
visualReviewNote: Professional Practice subject: no images needed. Text/audio-ready explanation only.

question: Plans, models and perspectives are?
keyMemory: Kapag may models and perspectives, isipin mo agad: presentation. Hindi agad contract documents.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Documents for presentation.
Ulitin natin: Plans, models, and perspectives are documents for presentation.
Ang clue dito ay kasama ang models and perspectives. Sa architectural practice, ang models at perspectives are commonly used to explain and present the design concept to the client, board, approving body, or public. Kaya ang best answer dito ay documents for presentation.
Yes, may plans na puwedeng maging part ng contract documents, lalo na working drawings or signed/sealed plans. Pero sa wording ng tanong, hindi niya sinabi “working drawings,” “specifications,” or “signed and sealed plans.” Ang kasama niya ay models and perspectives, kaya presentation ang direction ng question.
Bakit mali ang iba?
a. Instruments of practice — suspicious choice ito. Ang usual legal term is instruments of service, not instruments of practice. Kaya medyo trap ito.
c. Contract documents — hindi automatic. Models and perspectives are usually not contract documents unless expressly made part of the contract.
d. Project Requirements — too vague. Hindi ito proper classification.
Elimination method:
Tingnan mo ang words: plans, models, perspectives. Ang models and perspectives are visual presentation tools. So tanggal contract documents unless may sinabi na part sila ng agreement. Tanggal project requirements kasi vague. Tanggal instruments of practice kasi hindi standard term. Naiiwan ang documents for presentation.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag may models and perspectives, isipin mo agad: presentation. Hindi agad contract documents.

question: An architect refuses to give the original plan to the Owner. Why?

choices.text: The Owner is entitled only to the blueprint
The architect’s work is covered by intellectual property rights/law
In order to prevent the Owner from making duplicates and/or constructing without his knowledge

keyMemory: Architectural plans, specifications, and related documents are instruments of service and intellectual property of the architect. The owner may use them for the agreed project, but cannot freely duplicate, reuse, or construct another project from them without the architect’s written consent. Tama, tuloy-tuloy tayo sa numbering. Page 1 ended at 2, Page 2 ended at 8, so itong Page 3 starts at Question 9.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. All of the Above.
Ulitin natin: The correct answer is All of the Above.
Ito ang tamang sagot kasi under RA 9266, particularly the principle on ownership of plans, specifications, and other contract documents, the architect’s drawings, specifications, and similar documents are considered instruments of service and are the intellectual property and documents of the architect.
Ibig sabihin, kahit binayaran ng owner ang architectural services, hindi automatic na pagmamay-ari na niya ang original drawings in the sense na pwede niyang i-duplicate, gamitin sa ibang project, or gamitin for repetition without written consent. The owner is generally entitled to use the plans for the specific project agreed upon, but not to freely reproduce or reuse them.
Bakit tama ang bawat choice?
a. The Owner is entitled only to the blueprint — in older drafting language, ang point nito ay the owner receives copies needed for the project, not necessarily the architect’s original tracing, master copy, or original signed instruments.
b. The architect’s work is covered by intellectual property rights/law — yes. Architectural documents are protected as intellectual property and instruments of service.
c. To prevent duplicates or construction without the architect’s knowledge — yes. This is exactly the protection intended: hindi pwedeng gamitin ulit ang plans for another building or repetition without written consent.
So dahil all three are valid reasons, ang sagot ay d. All of the Above.
Elimination method:
Kapag may All of the Above, huwag mo agad piliin. Test mo muna isa-isa.
Is A reasonable? Yes, owner gets copies, not necessarily originals.
Is B legally correct? Yes, IP rights.
Is C consistent with RA 9266 Sec. 33? Yes, bawal unauthorized duplication or reuse.
Kung lahat may sense, saka mo piliin ang All of the Above.
Common trap dito:
Akala ng ibang examinee, “Owner nagbayad, so kanya lahat.” Hindi ganoon. Sa architecture, may distinction between ownership of the physical copy and intellectual property/authorship. The owner may use the documents for the project, pero the architect retains authorship and intellectual property rights.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Architectural plans, specifications, and related documents are instruments of service and intellectual property of the architect. The owner may use them for the agreed project, but cannot freely duplicate, reuse, or construct another project from them without the architect’s written consent.
Tama, tuloy-tuloy tayo sa numbering. Page 1 ended at 2, Page 2 ended at 8, so itong Page 3 starts at Question 9.

question: A plan is drawn by a company draftsman under the supervision of Registered and Licensed Architect. Who owns the said document?
keyMemory: Kapag architectural document is prepared under an RLA’s direct supervision, the professional authorship and responsibility belong to the Registered and Licensed Architect.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. the RLA.
Ulitin natin: the RLA — the Registered and Licensed Architect — owns the document.
Bakit? Kasi sa Philippine architectural practice, kapag architectural plans, drawings, specifications, and other contract documents are prepared by or under the direct supervision of a registered architect, the professional responsibility and authorship point to the architect. RA 9266 says the duly licensed architect affixes the seal on architectural documents prepared by or under his or her direct supervision, and the architect-of-record is fully responsible for documents issued under his or her seal. It also says signed, stamped, or sealed drawings and specifications are instruments of service and intellectual property of the architect. (Lawphil)
So kahit draftsman ang gumuhit physically, hindi siya ang professional author. Think of it this way: ang draftsman is assisting in production. Ang architect ang nag-supervise, nag-control ng design intent, at siya ang legally responsible.
Bakit mali ang iba?
a. draftsman — mali, kasi siya ang gumuhit physically, pero hindi siya ang registered professional author.
c. Company — possible na may internal employment arrangement, pero sa ALE logic, architectural document as instrument of service belongs to the architect/author, especially kung under RLA supervision and seal.
d. Client — mali. The client may receive copies and may use them for the agreed project, pero hindi automatic na kanya ang authorship or intellectual property.
Elimination method:
Hanapin mo ang keyword: under the supervision of Registered and Licensed Architect. Ibig sabihin may professional control. Tanggal agad draftsman kasi hindi siya RLA. Tanggal client kasi nagpagawa lang siya, hindi siya author. Tanggal company kasi hindi ito ang professional signatory sa tanong. Naiiwan ang RLA.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag architectural document is prepared under an RLA’s direct supervision, the professional authorship and responsibility belong to the Registered and Licensed Architect.
________________________________________

question: You had a residential project for a Client. After the construction, you found out that the Client used your design for another project without your consent. What will you do?

choices.text: File a case with the PRC

answer: D. File a case in court.
keyMemory: Kapag ginamit ulit ang design mo for another project without written consent, violation iyon ng ownership/intellectual property principle under RA 9266. Proper action sa choices: file a case in court.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. File a case in court.
Ulitin natin: File a case in court.
Ang key phrase dito ay used your design for another project without your consent. That is unauthorized duplication or reuse. Under RA 9266, architectural drawings, specifications, and contract documents are instruments of service and intellectual property of the architect. It is unlawful to duplicate or make copies of those documents for repetition or for other projects or buildings without the written consent of the architect or author. (Lawphil)
So ang issue dito is not just “nainis ako sa client.” Legal violation ito ng architect’s rights over the plans. Kaya hindi sapat na kausapin lang ang mayor, at hindi rin “forgive and forget.”
Bakit court, not PRC? Kasi ang offender sa question is the client, not necessarily a registered architect. PRC mainly regulates professionals and professional practice. If the issue is unauthorized use by the owner/client, the stronger remedy in the choices is to file a case in court. Kung registered architect naman ang nangopya or nag-misuse, puwede ring may PRC administrative angle. Pero sa given facts, client ang gumawa, kaya court ang best answer.
Bakit mali ang iba?
a. Forgive and forget — mali, kasi may violation of intellectual property/instruments of service.
b. Inform the Mayor — mali, kasi mayor is not the proper legal forum for enforcing architect’s ownership of design documents.
c. File a case with the PRC — not the best answer here because the violator is the client/owner, not necessarily a PRC-regulated architect.
d. File a case in court — tama, because unauthorized reuse can become a legal case.
Elimination method:
Keyword: without your consent. Kapag may unauthorized reuse, legal issue na iyon. Tanggal “forgive and forget.” Tanggal “inform the mayor” kasi hindi siya proper forum. Between PRC and court, tingnan mo sino ang offender: client. So court ang mas tama.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ginamit ulit ang design mo for another project without written consent, violation iyon ng ownership/intellectual property principle under RA 9266. Proper action sa choices: file a case in court.
________________________________________

question: Before submitting sketches to a client, what must the architect first do?
keyMemory: Before giving sketches, concepts, or design output, secure a written service agreement. Sa ALE, kapag may “before submitting” or “before rendering service,” sagot usually points to service agreement.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Sign a service agreement.
Ulitin natin: Before submitting sketches, sign a service agreement.
Ito ang very important professional practice rule. Under RA 9266, it is prohibited to force or require an architect to perform architectural services without first executing a written contract or service agreement. The law specifically mentions the need for a written contract/service agreement before undertaking services in the general practice of architecture. (Lawphil)
Bakit ito mahalaga? Kasi sketches are already architectural services. Hindi porke preliminary pa lang, libre na. Kapag nag-submit ka ng sketches without agreement, vulnerable ka: puwedeng gamitin ang idea mo, puwedeng hindi ka bayaran, and it weakens professional practice.
Bakit mali ang iba?
b. Bill for downpayment — downpayment is good practice, pero dapat may agreement muna kung ano ang scope, fee, terms, and obligations.
c. Discuss board for presentation — presentation step na ito, not the legal first step.
d. Prepare probable construction cost — part ito ng services, pero again, dapat may service agreement muna bago ka gumawa ng professional output.
Elimination method:
Keyword: before submitting sketches. Since sketches are already service output, kailangan muna ng written agreement. Tanggal all choices that are later tasks. Naiiwan ang Sign a service agreement.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Before giving sketches, concepts, or design output, secure a written service agreement. Sa ALE, kapag may “before submitting” or “before rendering service,” sagot usually points to service agreement.
________________________________________
visualReviewNote: Professional Practice subject: no images needed. Text/audio-ready explanation only.

question: What do you call a signed proposal?
keyMemory: Proposal plus acceptance/signature equals contract.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Contract.
Ulitin natin: a signed proposal becomes a contract.
Ang logic dito simple pero important sa professional practice. A proposal by itself is an offer. Kapag pinirmahan at tinanggap ng other party, nagkakaroon na ng agreement. So in board-exam language, a signed proposal is treated as a contract.
Bakit hindi Bid Document? Kasi bid document is usually part of bidding requirements or documents issued for pricing/competition. Hindi lahat ng signed proposal ay bid document.
Bakit hindi Financial Proposal? Kasi financial proposal focuses on fees or cost offer. Pero kapag signed and accepted, hindi na siya merely financial proposal; it becomes binding agreement.
Bakit hindi Billing Statement? Kasi billing statement is a request for payment. Hindi siya agreement for services.
Elimination method:
Hanapin ang keyword: signed. Kapag proposal is signed/accepted, nagiging binding agreement. Tanggal bid document kasi bidding context lang. Tanggal financial proposal kasi offer pa lang. Tanggal billing statement kasi collection document. Naiiwan ang Contract.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Proposal plus acceptance/signature equals contract.
________________________________________

question: A person found preparing architectural plans without the necessary license is guilty of what?
keyMemory: Unauthorized practice is illegal, yes, pero under RA 9266 Sec. 29, the board-exam legal answer is misdemeanor.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. misdemeanor.
Ulitin natin: guilty of misdemeanor.
Now, listen carefully dito kasi may trap. The act is indeed an illegal practice in ordinary language. But the legal wording under RA 9266 Section 29 says that a person practicing or offering to practice architecture without being registered/licensed shall be guilty of misdemeanor and charged in court by the Commission. (Lawphil)
So kung board exam ang tanong ay “guilty of what?”, hanapin mo yung legal phrase. Ang legal phrase is misdemeanor.
Bakit mali ang iba?
a. nothing — obviously mali. Preparing architectural plans without license is not allowed.
c. illegal practice — conceptually true, pero hindi ito ang exact statutory classification asked by “guilty of what.”
d. misrepresentation — mali unless the facts say he pretended to be someone else or used another architect’s name/title/seal.
Elimination method:
Keyword: without the necessary license. That points to RA 9266 Sec. 29. Then ask: ano ang exact phrase ng law? Guilty of misdemeanor. Kaya kahit tempting ang “illegal practice,” piliin ang misdemeanor.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Unauthorized practice is illegal, yes, pero under RA 9266 Sec. 29, the board-exam legal answer is misdemeanor.

question: What offense is committed when an architect is found using the seal of another architect?
keyMemory: Kapag ginamit ang seal, COR, PIC, or identity ng ibang architect, RA 9266 treats it as a misdemeanor.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. misdemeanor.
Ulitin natin: using the seal of another architect is a misdemeanor under RA 9266.
RA 9266 Section 29 includes using or attempting to use as one’s own the Certificate of Registration, Professional Identification Card, or seal of another. The same section says these acts are punishable as misdemeanor. (Lawphil)
Bakit hindi “misrepresentation”? Kasi yes, may misrepresentation element siya in common sense. Pero the question asks the offense under the law, and the section classifies it as misdemeanor.
Bakit mali ang iba?
a. nothing — mali, serious violation ito.
c. illegal practice — broad description, pero not the exact legal offense label being tested here.
d. misrepresentation — may flavor of misrepresentation, pero RA 9266’s tested phrase is misdemeanor.
Elimination method:
Look for the trigger: using the seal of another architect. That is directly listed in Sec. 29. Kapag Sec. 29 violation, ang memory answer: guilty of misdemeanor.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ginamit ang seal, COR, PIC, or identity ng ibang architect, RA 9266 treats it as a misdemeanor.
________________________________________

question: What offense is committed if a person uses the name of another architect in his practice without the consent of the latter?
keyMemory: For RA 9266 Sec. 29 questions, maraming acts ang mukhang “illegal practice” or “misrepresentation,” pero kapag tinanong guilty of what offense, the ALE answer is usually misdemeanor.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. misdemeanor.
Ulitin natin: the offense is misdemeanor.
Again, the act sounds like misrepresentation — and in everyday language, yes, nagpapanggap siya or ginagamit niya ang identity ng iba. Pero sa RA 9266 Section 29, falsely impersonating a registrant, using another’s identity, or assuming a title or description to convey that one is an architect when one is not, falls under the prohibited acts that make the person guilty of misdemeanor. (Lawphil)
Bakit mali ang iba?
a. nothing — mali, because using another architect’s name without consent is a violation.
c. illegal practice — broad term, but the specific statutory answer is misdemeanor.
d. misrepresentation — descriptive, but not the best board answer if the law’s exact label is being asked.
Elimination method:
Kapag question gives facts like using another architect’s name, seal, registration, or title, connect it to RA 9266 Sec. 29. Then remember the ending phrase: shall be guilty of misdemeanor. Kaya B.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
For RA 9266 Sec. 29 questions, maraming acts ang mukhang “illegal practice” or “misrepresentation,” pero kapag tinanong guilty of what offense, the ALE answer is usually misdemeanor.

question: The PRC will not issue the Certificate of Registration to a successful examinee if he commits one of the following:

choices.text: Sign his signature in a project that he didn’t design
Unethical practice by competing with other architects

keyMemory: Refusal to issue COR is about applicant-level disqualification: false application, moral turpitude, immoral or dishonorable conduct, or unsound mind.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. Immoral act.
Ulitin natin: PRC will not issue the Certificate of Registration for immoral act.
Ang basis nito is RA 9266 Section 22: the Board shall not register and issue a Certificate of Registration and Professional Identification Card to a person who has falsely sworn or misrepresented himself in the application, or has been convicted of a criminal offense involving moral turpitude, or is guilty of immoral and dishonorable conduct, or is of unsound mind. (Lawphil)
Sa choices, ang pinaka-direct match sa printed provision is immoral act.
Bakit mali ang iba?
a. Sign his signature in a project that he didn’t design — serious violation ito, pero mas connected ito sa suspension or revocation grounds after registration, especially signing/sealing work not prepared by him or under his immediate supervision. RA 9266 Section 23 covers that kind of situation. (Lawphil)
b. Unethical practice by competing with other architects — competition itself is not automatically a ground for refusal to issue COR. Unethical conduct must be specific and proven.
c. Fraudulent act — medyo tricky. Fraud can matter if it involves false swearing or misrepresentation in the application, pero as written, the visible reviewer text points more directly to immoral/dishonorable conduct.
d. Immoral act — ito ang direct match sa ground for refusal.
Elimination method:
Keyword: will not issue Certificate of Registration. That points to Sec. 22, not Sec. 23. Sec. 22 grounds include moral turpitude, immoral/dishonorable conduct, unsound mind, and misrepresentation in application. Among the choices, Immoral act is the clearest match.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Refusal to issue COR is about applicant-level disqualification: false application, moral turpitude, immoral or dishonorable conduct, or unsound mind.

question: An Architect is employed in a certain government agency. The head of the agency commissioned him to prepare the designs and documents for an office building. The Architect receives annual compensation of ₱450,000. If the professional fee is pegged at 7% of the ₱50 million project construction cost, how much should the Architect ask as additional compensation?
keyMemory: Kapag government-employed architect at additional compensation, huwag agad 7% of project cost. Check the cap: not more than 50% of annual salary.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. ₱225,000.00.
Ulitin natin: ₱225,000.00 ang additional compensation.
Ito yung may calculation, pero may legal cap. Kung straight percentage lang, 7% of ₱50,000,000 is ₱3,500,000. Pero hindi iyon ang final answer dahil government-employed architect siya. Under the IRR provision cited in review references, the amount for the government architect may be based on the project cost but shall not exceed 50% of his annual salary, payable upon completion. (Architecture Board)
So compute tayo slowly:
Annual compensation = ₱450,000
Maximum additional compensation = 50% of ₱450,000
50% × ₱450,000 = ₱225,000
Kaya ang sagot ay ₱225,000.00.
Bakit mali ang iba?
a. ₱750,000 — walang basis sa given numbers.
b. ₱3,500,000 — ito ang trap. Ito ang 7% ng ₱50 million, pero lumampas ito sa allowed cap.
c. Nothing — mukhang plausible kasi government employee siya, pero sa specific rule na tinuturo ng question, may allowable additional compensation subject to the 50% cap.
d. ₱225,000 — ito ang 50% ng annual salary.
Elimination method:
Una, huwag agad magpadala sa 7%. Board exam trap iyan. Check kung may cap. Since government architect siya, hanapin ang 50% of annual salary. Kaya tanggal ₱3.5M kahit mathematically correct sa 7%, kasi legally capped.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag government-employed architect at additional compensation, huwag agad 7% of project cost. Check the cap: not more than 50% of annual salary.

question: Big scale projects sometimes have a lot of phases and areas that may require help from numerous architects and consultants per phase. A concept architect does the overall concept of the project. What is his liability on the project?

choices.text: He has civil and administrative liability
They all share the same amount of liability

keyMemory: Sa malaking project, hindi lahat ng architect ay pare-pareho ang liability. Ang Architect-of-Record ang may civil liability sa signed and sealed contract documents. Ang concept architect, kung hindi siya AOR, does not automatically carry that same liability.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. He has no liability.
Ulitin natin: He has no liability — in the sense na hindi siya ang Architect-of-Record liability holder.
Important nuance ito. Sa board exam logic, ang Concept Architect is not automatically the Architect-of-Record. Under RA 9266, the Architect-of-Record is the one directly and professionally responsible for the total design and assumes civil liability for the plans, specifications, and contract documents he or she signed and sealed. Also, under the IRR, a Consulting Architect does not assume Article 1723 civil liability unless he interferes with the legal and professional functions of the Architect-of-Record, although he may still have normal contractual liability under his own service agreement. (Lawphil)
Kaya ang ibig sabihin ng sagot na “He has no liability” dito ay: wala siyang AOR-type civil liability over the whole project kung hindi siya ang nag-sign and seal as Architect-of-Record.
Bakit mali ang iba?
a. Same as the architect-on-record — mali, kasi iba ang role ng concept architect sa AOR. Ang AOR ang may total professional responsibility sa signed and sealed documents.
c. He has civil and administrative liability — too broad sa context ng tanong. May possible contractual responsibility siya sa scope niya, pero hindi iyon ang hinahanap ng item.
d. They all share the same amount of liability — mali. Hindi pantay-pantay ang liability. Depende ito sa role, scope, contract, supervision, at kung sino ang nag-sign and seal.
Elimination method:
Hanapin mo ang keyword: concept architect. Hindi sinabi na siya ang Architect-of-Record, hindi sinabi na siya ang nag-sign and seal, at hindi sinabi na siya ang architect-in-charge of construction. Kaya huwag mong bigyan ng same liability as AOR. Tanggal A and D. Between B and C, ang mas board-exam answer is B, kasi ang tinutukoy ay liability over the project as AOR.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa malaking project, hindi lahat ng architect ay pare-pareho ang liability. Ang Architect-of-Record ang may civil liability sa signed and sealed contract documents. Ang concept architect, kung hindi siya AOR, does not automatically carry that same liability.

question: Three siblings decided to form their own architectural firm. One is an Architect, another is an Engineer and the last is an Interior Designer. What kind of business organization will they form?
keyMemory: Kapag several individuals form a professional firm together and walang corporation clue, think partnership.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. Partnership.
Ulitin natin: Partnership.
Tatlo sila. So hindi puwedeng single proprietorship, kasi single owner iyon. Hindi rin automatic corporation, kasi walang sinabi na incorporated sila, may shares of stock, board of directors, or SEC incorporation setup. Since magkakasamang individuals sila forming a firm, ang pinaka-natural at ALE answer dito ay partnership.
Bakit mali ang iba?
a. Single Proprietorship — mali agad. Isa lang ang owner dito dapat.
b. Conglomeration — hindi ito usual legal business organization for a professional architectural firm.
c. Corporation — possible in real life kung mag-incorporate sila, pero walang clue na corporation ang structure.
d. Partnership — tama, kasi multiple professionals forming a firm together.
Elimination method:
Keyword: three siblings decided to form their own firm. More than one person equals not single proprietorship. No corporation clues. Conglomeration is too big and not standard. Naiiwan ang partnership.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag several individuals form a professional firm together and walang corporation clue, think partnership.

question: If they are to apply for a business permit, where should they go?
keyMemory: Business permit or mayor’s permit = City Hall / LGU, not DTI, not SEC, not BIR.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. City Hall.
Ulitin natin: City Hall.
Ang keyword dito ay business permit. Hindi ito business name registration. Hindi rin ito tax registration. A business permit or mayor’s permit is obtained from the local government, usually through the City or Municipal Hall, specifically the Business Permits and Licensing Office. DTI also explains that business name registration only gives the business legal identity; to actually operate, you still need a Business or Mayor’s Permit. (BNRS)
Bakit mali ang iba?
a. SEC — partnership or corporation registration ito, not the business permit itself.
b. DTI — business name registration ito, especially for sole proprietorship, not the local operating permit.
d. BIR — tax registration ito, not the mayor’s/business permit.
c. City Hall — tama, kasi doon kinukuha ang local business permit.
Elimination method:
Kapag tanong ay register business organization, isipin mo DTI or SEC. Pero kapag tanong ay business permit, isipin mo City Hall.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Business permit or mayor’s permit = City Hall / LGU, not DTI, not SEC, not BIR.

question: A contractor has a son who was born in the Philippines but was raised and educated in New York. His son, John, is a licensed architect in New York. Upon his father’s prodding, Arch. John returned to the Philippines to be with his father. However, instead of joining his father’s firm, he decided to set up his own firm. The son’s firm submitted drawings to the OBO for building permit but the drawings were rejected. What went wrong?

choices.text: John’s firm is not registered with the DTI/SEC
Arch. John is not allowed to practice Architecture in the Philippines
John’s firm is not registered with the PRC
He did not study architecture in the Philippines

answer: B. Arch. John is not allowed to practice Architecture in the Philippines.
keyMemory: Foreign license is not automatically valid for Philippine architectural practice. Local practice needs Philippine licensure or proper temporary/special permit.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Arch. John is not allowed to practice Architecture in the Philippines.
Ulitin natin: Arch. John is not allowed to practice Architecture in the Philippines.
Ito ang tamang sagot kasi being licensed in New York does not automatically authorize a person to practice architecture in the Philippines. RA 9266 says that no person shall practice architecture in the Philippines, prepare architectural plans, or use the title architect in the local practice unless registered and licensed under Philippine law, subject only to proper temporary/special permit rules for foreign architects. (Architecture Board)
So kahit licensed siya abroad, kailangan pa rin ng Philippine authority: either local registration/licensure, or if applicable, proper temporary/special permit under PRC/PRBoA rules.
Bakit mali ang iba?
a. Not registered with DTI/SEC — possible business issue ito, pero hindi ito ang main reason drawings for building permit are rejected. The bigger issue is professional authority to sign and seal architectural documents.
c. Not registered with PRC — medyo malapit, pero mas complete ang B: hindi siya allowed to practice architecture in the Philippines.
d. Did not study architecture in the Philippines — mali. Hindi required na sa Pilipinas ka nag-aral, but you must meet Philippine licensure/permit requirements.
Elimination method:
Keyword: licensed architect in New York plus submitted drawings to OBO in the Philippines. Ang trap dito: foreign license does not equal Philippine right to practice. Tanggal D kasi education location is not the main issue. Tanggal A kasi business registration is secondary. Between B and C, mas direct at mas complete ang B.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Foreign license is not automatically valid for Philippine architectural practice. Local practice needs Philippine licensure or proper temporary/special permit.

question: The son’s firm submitted drawings to the OBO for building permit but the drawings were rejected. What can be done to remedy the problem?

choices.text: Hire a local Architect to sign and seal for him
Determine if a reciprocity agreement exist between the Philippines and New York
Pass the local Licensure Examination for Architects
File a case against the OBO

answer: C. Pass the local Licensure Examination for Architects.
keyMemory: Never use a local architect as a mere “signing architect.” Proper remedy: become legally authorized to practice, usually by passing the local ALE if qualified.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Pass the local Licensure Examination for Architects.
Ulitin natin: Pass the local Licensure Examination for Architects.
Ito ang proper remedy kung gusto niyang mag-practice as an architect in the Philippines on his own, lalo na kung Filipino citizen siya or qualified applicant siya. Hindi sapat na licensed siya sa New York. Kailangan niyang maging properly registered and licensed under Philippine law before he can independently prepare, sign, and seal architectural documents for local building permit purposes. RA 9266 requires Philippine registration/licensure for local practice, and architectural documents must be signed and sealed by a registered and licensed architect under the Act. (Architecture Board)
Bakit mali ang iba?
a. Hire a local Architect to sign and seal for him — malaking trap ito. Hindi puwedeng taga-pirma lang ang local architect. Signing and sealing drawings not prepared by the architect or under his direct supervision is unethical and punishable.
b. Determine reciprocity agreement — relevant ito mainly for foreign architects seeking temporary/special practice or foreign applicants under reciprocity rules. Pero sa given choices, if John wants his own Philippine practice, the clean remedy is local licensure.
d. File a case against the OBO — mali. The OBO is correct to reject documents not compliant with Philippine professional requirements.
Elimination method:
Tanong: paano niya maaayos para makapag-practice legally? Hindi by shortcut signing. Hindi by suing the OBO. Reciprocity may be relevant only in special foreign-practice cases. The direct and proper route is pass the local licensure examination.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Never use a local architect as a mere “signing architect.” Proper remedy: become legally authorized to practice, usually by passing the local ALE if qualified.

question: What is a successful Architect?

explanation:
Correct answer: c. Good workmanship and ethical.

Successful architect sa professional ethics = competent, responsible, and ethical, not merely famous or maraming projects.

question: Allan, a 5th year architecture student is commissioned by Mr. & Mrs. ABC to design a house. Allan had the plans signed by his professor Architect Petmalu. Who is unethical?
keyMemory: Hindi puwedeng gawing “pirmahan mo na lang, Architect” ang architectural documents. Ang seal means responsibility, supervision, and authorship.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. All of them.
Ulitin natin: All of them.
Dito, broad ethics ang tinatanong, hindi pa PRC penalty. Kaya lahat sila may participation sa maling setup.
Si Allan, 5th year student pa lang, so hindi pa siya Registered and Licensed Architect. Hindi siya dapat tumanggap ng commission to prepare architectural plans as if he can legally practice architecture.
Si Arch. Petmalu, mas mabigat ang violation. Bakit? Kasi licensed architect siya. Alam niya dapat na hindi puwedeng pirmahan at selyuhan ang plans na hindi niya ginawa o hindi under his direct supervision. Ang seal is not a decoration. Ang seal means professional responsibility.
Si Mr. and Mrs. ABC, as clients, naging part sila ng improper arrangement. Nagpa-design sila sa hindi licensed, then ginamit ang professor para ma-legitimize ang documents.
Pero important distinction: sa ethics sense, all of them are wrong. Sa PRC disciplinary sense, ang PRC can directly discipline the licensed professional, which is the professor. Lalabas iyan sa next question.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. Mr. & Mrs. ABC only — kulang. Hindi lang client ang mali.
b. Allan only — kulang. Mas mabigat pa nga ang violation ng professor dahil licensed siya.
c. Allan and Arch. Petmalu — malakas itong choice, pero sa broad ethical question, kasama rin ang client dahil they participated in an improper arrangement.
d. All of them — best answer dito.
Elimination method:
Kapag may scenario na student gumagawa ng plans, client nag-commission, tapos licensed architect pumirma lang, tanungin mo: “Sino ang nakinabang o nakilahok sa maling proseso?” Lahat sila. Kaya All of them.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Hindi puwedeng gawing “pirmahan mo na lang, Architect” ang architectural documents. Ang seal means responsibility, supervision, and authorship.

question: Who can be reprimanded by the PRC?
keyMemory: Ethically, several people can be wrong. But for PRC reprimand, focus on the registered professional.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Arch. Petmalu.
Ulitin natin: Arch. Petmalu ang can be reprimanded by the PRC.
Ito ang distinction from the previous question. Sa Question 45, ethical wrongdoing ang pinag-usapan. Pero dito, PRC reprimand na ang tanong.

Pero si Arch. Petmalu, registered architect siya. He allowed his professional seal and signature to be used on work that was not properly his, or not properly under his direct supervision. Kaya siya ang may professional liability before PRC.
Bakit mali ang iba?
b. Allan and Mr./Mrs. ABC — hindi sila PRC-registered architects.
c. Allan and Arch. Petmalu — si Allan may possible legal issue, pero PRC reprimand as a professional applies to the licensed architect.
d. All of them — wrong for PRC reprimand. PRC cannot reprimand everyone as if all are licensed professionals.
a. Arch. Petmalu — tama.
Elimination method:
Keyword: reprimanded by the PRC. Ang unang tanong mo dapat: “Sino dito ang licensed professional under PRC?” Only Arch. Petmalu.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Ethically, several people can be wrong. But for PRC reprimand, focus on the registered professional.

question: A 5th year Architecture student is asked and contracted by his aunt to design a 2 storey residence for her. After the student finished preparing all the architectural plans, he asked his professor to sign and seal it. The professor agrees to do it for a sum of Php 6,000.00. Is there anything wrong with this practice? If yes, who is liable? What is the case if ever?

choices.text: Nothing is wrong since everybody involved mutually agreed on it
Yes, estafa for Allan who was contracted by his aunt
Yes, violation of intellectual property rights on the part of the professor
Yes, violation of the Code of Ethics by the professor

answer: D. Yes, violation of the Code of Ethics by the professor.
keyMemory: Ang seal ng architect ay hindi binebenta. Kapag pumirma at nagselyo ka, inaako mo ang professional responsibility.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. Yes, violation of the Code of Ethics by the professor.
Ulitin natin: Yes, violation of the Code of Ethics by the professor.
Ito halos kapareho ng previous scenario. Ang student ang gumawa ng plans, pero professor ang pumirma at nagselyo, tapos may bayad pang ₱6,000.
Ang mali dito: the professor used his professional authority improperly. Hindi puwedeng ibenta ang pirma at seal. Hindi puwedeng “sign and seal service” lang siya. Kapag architect ka at nagselyo ka, sinasabi mo sa public, sa OBO, sa client, at sa profession na ikaw ang responsible professional over those documents.
Bakit hindi nothing is wrong? Kahit agreed lahat, hindi ibig sabihin legal or ethical. Consent of parties does not legalize unethical professional practice.
Bakit hindi estafa for Allan? Estafa requires deceit and damage in a specific legal sense. Dito, hindi iyon ang main issue being tested. The board exam wants professional ethics.
Bakit hindi violation of intellectual property rights by professor? Hindi IP ang main issue. Ang main violation ay improper signing and sealing of documents.
Bakit violation of Code of Ethics by professor? Kasi siya ang licensed architect. Siya ang may professional duty not to sign/seal documents not prepared by him or under his responsible direction.
Elimination method:
Tanggal agad A, kasi “mutual agreement” does not excuse illegal or unethical practice.
Tanggal B, kasi estafa is not the core professional practice issue.
Tanggal C, kasi hindi IP rights ang central violation.
Naiiwan D: Code of Ethics violation by the professor.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Ang seal ng architect ay hindi binebenta. Kapag pumirma at nagselyo ka, inaako mo ang professional responsibility.

question: When can an architect perform his own design?
keyMemory: Kapag architect designs and also undertakes implementation/construction under a proper arrangement, think design-build.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. Under design-built services.

Ang ibig sabihin ng “perform his own design” dito ay hindi lang gumawa ng design, kundi i-implement or i-construct ang project based on his own design.
Sa normal setup, separate ang designer at contractor. Pero may recognized arrangement called design-build, where the architect may be involved in both design and construction responsibility, basta proper ang agreement, transparent sa client, at aligned sa standards of professional practice.
Bakit hindi as a contractor? Kasi too broad. Kung basta contractor lang, pwedeng magkaroon ng conflict or improper professional arrangement. Ang tamang professional service classification is design-build.
Bakit hindi as construction manager? Construction manager manages time, cost, quality, coordination, but does not necessarily construct his own design.
Bakit hindi under full-time construction supervision? Supervision is checking and monitoring construction, not necessarily performing or undertaking the construction.
Bakit design-build services? Kasi iyon ang recognized arrangement where design and construction delivery are integrated.
Elimination method:
Keyword: perform his own design. Ibig sabihin may design plus execution. Among the choices, ang may design plus build ay design-build services.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag architect designs and also undertakes implementation/construction under a proper arrangement, think design-build.

question: What is the foremost role of an architect with regards to his client?
keyMemory: To the client, the architect’s foremost role is professional adviser — may good faith, judgment, and responsibility.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Adviser.
Ulitin natin: The foremost role of an architect to the client is adviser.
Sa client relationship, ang architect is not merely a draftsman. Hindi rin siya ordinary employee ng client. The architect gives professional judgment. He advises the client on design, planning, budget implications, site issues, code compliance, construction methods, and professional decisions.
Kaya ang architect must act in good faith. He protects the client’s interest, pero not at the expense of public safety, public welfare, or professional integrity.
Bakit mali ang iba?
b. Design Manager — possible role ito in a project team, pero not the foremost ethical role to the client.
c. Design Consultant — close, pero narrower. Adviser is broader and more professional.
d. Employee — wrong. Even when hired, the architect is a professional adviser, not merely a subordinate who follows anything the client wants.
Elimination method:
Kapag tanong ay with regards to his client, hanapin ang role na may trust, judgment, and guidance. Iyon ay Adviser.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
To the client, the architect’s foremost role is professional adviser — may good faith, judgment, and responsibility.

question: What is the role of the architect between the client and contractor?

choices.text: To execute judicial orders with impartiality

answer: B. To execute judicial orders with impartiality.
keyMemory: To the client, the architect is an adviser. Between client and contractor, the architect must act with impartiality in contract administration.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. To execute judicial orders with impartiality.
Ulitin natin: to execute judicial orders with impartiality — pero mas tama itong intindihin as quasi-judicial or impartial decision-making role.
Medyo awkward ang wording ng choice. Hindi ibig sabihin na judge ang architect sa court. Ang ibig sabihin nito: between client and contractor, the architect must act fairly and impartially when interpreting contract documents, evaluating work, certifying payments, checking compliance, and resolving project matters within his professional authority.
The architect is hired by the client, yes. Pero during construction administration, hindi siya dapat unfair sa contractor. Dapat he safeguards the client’s interest while also treating the contractor fairly according to the contract documents.
Bakit hindi mediator? Medyo close siya, pero too general. The more professional answer is impartial decision-maker under the contract administration role.
Bakit hindi consultant? Consultant is too broad and does not capture the fairness between client and contractor.
Bakit hindi adviser? Adviser is more for the client relationship. Between client and contractor, impartiality ang keyword.
Bakit B? Dahil may word na impartiality. Iyan ang clue. Sa ganitong tanong, kapag may choice na may impartiality, usually iyon ang hinahanap.
Elimination method:
Question says between client and contractor. So hanapin ang role na hindi one-sided.
Adviser? More for client.
Consultant? Too broad.
Mediator? Close but weak.
Choice B has impartiality, kaya iyon ang strongest answer.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
To the client, the architect is an adviser. Between client and contractor, the architect must act with impartiality in contract administration.

explanation:
Correct answer: a. Architect.
Ulitin natin: Architect ang dapat unang mag-sorry.
Bakit architect? Kasi as architect, responsibility mo na bantayan kung ang materials, equipment, or workmanship ay sumusunod sa contract documents and specifications. Kapag nakita mo na substandard or hindi ayon sa specs, dapat immediately mong i-reject or i-condemn, in a clear and timely way.
Ang naging problema sa scenario: parang nahuli ang reaction ng architect. The materials were already used and installed, then saka nag-refuse to sign the billing. Dahil doon, nagkaroon ng stoppage, anger, dispute, and possible delay cost.
Hindi ibig sabihin na mali ang architect dahil ni-reject niya ang substandard materials. Tama iyon. Ang mali ay kung hindi niya ito na-address agad, clearly, and professionally.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
b. Owner — may fault din ang owner kasi siya ang nag-insist na siya ang mag-supply ng materials, and apparently substandard ang na-supply niya. Pero the question is asking who should say sorry first in terms of professional handling of the issue. The architect should have acted early and clearly.
c. Contractor — the contractor installed the materials, yes. Pero kung owner-supplied materials iyon, and walang timely rejection from architect, the contractor may argue na ginamit lang niya ang provided materials.
d. No one. Let’s settle this in court — too aggressive. Board exam trap ito. Hindi lahat ng dispute, court agad. Professional practice expects coordination, clarification, timely rejection, and dispute prevention.
Elimination method:
Tingnan mo ang clue: substandard materials were noticed, but work already progressed and billing issue caused stoppage. Ang architect ang may duty to inspect, reject, and communicate promptly. So tanggal court agad. Tanggal owner and contractor as first apology because the architect is the professional administrator of the contract documents.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag may non-conforming materials, the architect must act immediately, clearly, and professionally. Huwag hintayin na lumaki ang problema bago mag-reject.

question: As a RLA, you made a very good working relationship with Contractor ABC after awarding them several projects. Now, Contractor ABC wanted your services as the Architect for their new building. All things went smoothly until the Client wanted you to alter your plans to accommodate additional requirements. You implemented their revisions but the Client was not satisfied. They requested another set of changes but you disagreed since the alterations being requested will result to violations with the local zoning regulations and the building code. This eventually leads to a dispute between you and the client. Based on the Architect’s Code of Ethical Conduct, you have a responsibility to whom?
keyMemory: Kapag ang request ng client violates law, zoning, building code, or public safety, the architect’s higher duty is to the Public.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. To the Public.
Ulitin natin: your responsibility is to the Public.
Ito ang important professional ethics principle: yes, may responsibility ka sa client. Yes, may professional relationship ka sa contractor. Pero kapag ang hinihingi ng client ay magiging violation ng zoning regulations or building code, mas mataas ang duty mo sa public safety, public welfare, and law.
Hindi puwedeng sabihin ng architect, “Client ko naman siya, susundin ko na lang.” Hindi ganoon. Architect ka, hindi ka draftsman na tagasunod lang ng gusto ng client. May legal and ethical obligation ka to obey building laws, zoning rules, safety requirements, and public welfare standards.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
b. To the Client — may duty ka sa client, pero hindi above the law. Hindi mo pwedeng protektahan ang client kung ang gusto niya ay code violation.
c. To the Contractor — may relationship ka sa contractor, pero hindi siya ang highest duty sa ganitong issue.
d. To Colleagues — important din ang professional respect, pero hindi ito ang issue dito.
Elimination method:
Keyword: violations with local zoning regulations and building code. Kapag may code violation, ang answer usually points to public welfare. So kahit client ang may request, hindi client ang highest answer. Piliin ang Public.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ang request ng client violates law, zoning, building code, or public safety, the architect’s higher duty is to the Public.
________________________________________
visualReviewNote: Professional Practice subject: no images needed. Text/audio-ready explanation only.

question: Where should the client go if they wanted to file a complaint against you?
keyMemory: Professional complaint against an architect: PRC-PRBOA.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. PRC-PRBOA.
Ulitin natin: PRC-PRBOA.
Kapag complaint against a Registered and Licensed Architect ang usapan, especially professional misconduct, unethical practice, or violation of RA 9266, ang proper professional regulatory body is the Professional Regulation Commission and the Professional Regulatory Board of Architecture.
Bakit hindi UAP-IAPOA? Kasi UAP-IAPOA is the accredited professional organization. It may have internal professional mechanisms, pero licensing and disciplinary authority belongs to PRC and PRBOA.
Bakit hindi RTC? RTC is court. Puwede ang court kung civil or criminal case ang issue, pero kung professional complaint against an architect, una mong isipin PRC-PRBOA.
Bakit hindi NBI? NBI is for investigation of crimes. Hindi siya standard venue for professional administrative complaint.
Elimination method:
Keyword: complaint against you as architect. Sino may power over architect’s license? PRC-PRBOA. Tanggal UAP as professional organization only. Tanggal RTC unless court case. Tanggal NBI unless criminal investigation.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Professional complaint against an architect: PRC-PRBOA.
________________________________________
visualReviewNote: Professional Practice subject: no images needed. Text/audio-ready explanation only.

question: Who should initiate the first move to settle this dispute?
keyMemory: In professional disputes, the architect should be proactive. Mag-init man ang situation, the RLA should initiate clear and professional settlement.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. The RLA.
Ulitin natin: The RLA should initiate the first move.
As the professional, the architect should take the responsible first step to clarify, communicate, and attempt to settle the dispute professionally. Hindi ibig sabihin aamin ka agad na mali ka. Ang ibig sabihin: ikaw ang trained professional, ikaw ang may ethical duty to avoid unnecessary conflict and protect public interest, client interest, and professional integrity.
Kapag dispute na ang nangyayari, hindi dapat pride ang pinapairal. Dapat documentation, explanation, meeting, clarification, and professional settlement.
Bakit mali ang iba?
a. Contractor ABC — possible party siya, pero the question is about the architect’s ethical conduct. The RLA should show leadership.
c. Hire a mediator — mediation can be useful later, pero hindi ito first move agad. The architect should first initiate clarification and settlement.
d. No one — mali. Kapag walang kikilos, lalala ang dispute.
Elimination method:
Keyword: settle this dispute. Since architect ang professional in question, and professional ethics ang context, the answer is the RLA. Hindi court agad, hindi mediator agad, hindi no one.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
In professional disputes, the architect should be proactive. Mag-init man ang situation, the RLA should initiate clear and professional settlement.
________________________________________
visualReviewNote: Professional Practice subject: no images needed. Text/audio-ready explanation only.

question: Who is responsible to orient the contractor to fully understand the contract document?
keyMemory: The architect must help the contractor understand the contract documents clearly, para maiwasan ang mistakes, delay, and extra cost.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Architect.
Ulitin natin: the Architect is responsible.
Ito mismo ang nasa principle ng relation with the contractor: the architect should give the contractor every reasonable assistance to enable him to fully understand the contents of the contract documents.
Bakit architect? Kasi siya ang author or professional interpreter ng drawings, specifications, and design intent. Siya ang nakakaintindi kung bakit ganoon ang plans, ano ang required sa specs, at paano dapat basahin ang contract documents.
Bakit mali ang iba?
a. Owner — owner may explain project goals, pero hindi siya ang professional interpreter ng drawings and specifications.
c. Construction Supervisor — may site role siya, pero hindi necessarily siya ang responsible to explain the contract documents in their professional design intent.
d. Project Manager — may coordination role, pero in architectural professional practice, the architect has the duty to clarify the contract documents to avoid mistakes and unnecessary costs.
Elimination method:
Keyword: fully understand the contract document. Sino ang may professional authorship and interpretation role? Architect.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
The architect must help the contractor understand the contract documents clearly, para maiwasan ang mistakes, delay, and extra cost.
