visualReviewNote: Professional Practice subject: no images needed. Text/audio-ready explanation only.

question: A client wants you to be his Architect but you found out there is another architect involved. What will you do?
keyMemory: Before replacing or taking over from another architect, clarify the status of the first architect’s engagement, termination, and compensation.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. Clarify with the client.
Ulitin natin: Clarify with the client.
Ito ang professional and ethical first step. Hindi ka agad tatanggap. Hindi ka rin agad aayaw. At lalong hindi ka agad magko-complain sa PRC. Una, alamin mo muna: active pa ba ang previous architect? Terminated na ba ang services niya? Nababayaran na ba siya? May proper turnover ba? May conflict ba?
Under ethical practice, an architect should not undertake a commission if he knows another architect was previously employed, unless the previous engagement has been properly terminated and duly compensated.
Bakit mali ang iba?
a. Proceed with the Project — mali. Baka may existing architect pa. Magiging unethical ka kung papasok ka without clarification.
b. Forego with the Project — too early. Baka naman properly terminated na ang previous architect. Kailangan muna i-clarify.
c. Complain to the PRC — walang basis yet. Hindi pa malinaw kung may violation.
d. Clarify with the client — tama. Ito ang first professional move.
Elimination method:
Kapag may another architect involved, huwag agad pumasok. Huwag rin agad tumanggi. Ang safest and ethical first move: clarify.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Before replacing or taking over from another architect, clarify the status of the first architect’s engagement, termination, and compensation.
________________________________________
visualReviewNote: Professional Practice subject: no images needed. Text/audio-ready explanation only.

question: When is the time when the owner can get replacement for his architect?

choices.text: Immediately without notifying the first architect
Immediately after terminating the services of the first architect
Within 15 days after terminating the services of the first architect
After terminating the services of the first architect and paying all obligations

answer: D. After terminating the services of the first architect and paying all obligations.
keyMemory: A client may replace an architect only after proper termination and settlement of obligations. Incoming architect must avoid taking over improperly.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. After terminating the services of the first architect and paying all obligations.
Ulitin natin: after terminating the services of the first architect and paying all obligations.
Ito ang proper and ethical rule. Hindi sapat na sabihin lang ng owner, “Ayaw ko na sa architect ko.” Kailangan maayos ang termination. At kung may unpaid professional fees, reimbursables, or obligations, dapat bayaran muna or properly settle.
This protects the first architect from being unfairly replaced after doing work. It also protects the incoming architect from stepping into an unethical situation.
Bakit mali ang iba?
a. Immediately without notifying the first architect — clearly unethical. Parang tinatanggal mo ang first architect behind his back.
b. Immediately after terminating the services — kulang. Termination alone is not enough. Dapat settled ang obligations.
c. Within 15 days after terminating — walang ganitong general rule sa choices. The issue is not number of days; the issue is proper termination and payment.
d. After terminating and paying all obligations — tama. Complete and ethical.
Elimination method:
Keyword: replacement for his architect. Hanapin mo ang answer na protects the first architect’s rights and prevents unethical takeover. The complete answer is termination plus payment of obligations.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
A client may replace an architect only after proper termination and settlement of obligations. Incoming architect must avoid taking over improperly.
visualReviewNote: Professional Practice subject: no images needed. Text/audio-ready explanation only.

question: A very discreet client has chosen you as the architect of his new project. Midway through the design phase, another architect has claimed that he was the rightful designer of the project and he was being consulted by the clients long before your agreement with the said client has taken place. Knowing about the discreet nature of the client, what should you do to settle this dispute?

choices.text: Suspend the project immediately and let the owner settle his matter with the former architect
Continue the project; ignore the other architect without giving another thought
Continue the project but help the owner in settlement with the third party
Suspend the project and help the owner settle with the third party

answer: D. Suspend the project and help the owner settle with the third party.
keyMemory: Kapag may prior architect or authorship dispute, pause the work, clarify, and help settle professionally. Huwag magpatuloy habang unresolved ang professional conflict.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. Suspend the project and help the owner settle with the third party.
Ulitin natin: Suspend the project and help the owner settle with the third party.
Bakit ito ang tama? Kasi may claim na may naunang architect na involved. As a professional, hindi ka puwedeng basta magpatuloy na parang walang nangyari. May possible issue ng prior engagement, intellectual property, authorship, at unpaid professional services.
Pero hindi rin tama na iiwan mo na lang ang owner na mag-isa. As the present architect, professional move ang tumulong sa clarification and settlement. So the best answer is not just “suspend,” but suspend and help settle.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. Suspend the project immediately and let the owner settle his matter with the former architect — kulang ito. Tama ang suspend, pero passive ka masyado. Professional ka sa situation, so dapat tumulong ka sa maayos na settlement.
b. Continue the project; ignore the other architect — mali. This is dangerous and unethical. Kapag may prior architect claim, ignoring it may lead to professional misconduct or intellectual property conflict.
c. Continue the project but help the owner in settlement — mali kasi habang unresolved ang dispute, risky magpatuloy. Baka ginagamit mo pala ang ideas or work ng naunang architect.
d. Suspend the project and help the owner settle with the third party — tama. Ito ang balanced, ethical, and professional response.
Elimination method:
Kapag may another architect claiming rightful design, huwag magpatuloy agad. Tanggal agad B and C kasi they continue the project. Between A and D, piliin D kasi hindi mo lang iiwan ang owner; tutulungan mo siyang ayusin ang dispute.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag may prior architect or authorship dispute, pause the work, clarify, and help settle professionally. Huwag magpatuloy habang unresolved ang professional conflict.
________________________________________
visualReviewNote: Professional Practice subject: no images needed. Text/audio-ready explanation only.

question: A client wants your design to be the same with another Architect. What should you do?
keyMemory: Client preference can inspire discussion, but you must produce an original design, not a copy of another architect’s work.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Do a different design.
Ulitin natin: Do a different design.
Ito ang tamang sagot kasi hindi puwedeng kopyahin ang design ng ibang architect. Architectural design is intellectual property. Kahit sabihin ng client, “Gusto ko yung ganito, gayahin mo,” hindi ibig sabihin pwede mo na siyang kopyahin.
Ang role mo as architect is to understand the client’s preference, then create an original solution. Puwede mong alamin ang gusto niyang character, mood, function, or planning idea, pero hindi mo kokopyahin ang design expression ng iba.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. Use the same design — obvious na mali. Direct copying ito.
b. Forego with the project — too extreme agad. Kung kaya mo namang i-educate ang client and make an original design, hindi kailangang umalis agad.
d. Adapt only the design concept — medyo trap ito. “Concept” sounds harmless, pero kung ang meaning ay kukunin pa rin ang intellectual idea ng ibang architect, delikado pa rin. Better answer is create a different design.
c. Do a different design — tama, kasi it respects the other architect’s work and protects your own professionalism.
Elimination method:
Keyword: same with another Architect. Kapag “same,” danger word yan. Tanggal A. Tanggal D kung it still relies on another architect’s design concept. Tanggal B kung may ethical way pa to continue. Naiiwan C.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Client preference can inspire discussion, but you must produce an original design, not a copy of another architect’s work.
________________________________________
visualReviewNote: Professional Practice subject: no images needed. Text/audio-ready explanation only.

question: As the Architect of a project, you have been instructed by a persistent client that a design he saw in another building should be copied. What can you do to tell the client that this kind of practice is unethical?

choices.text: Follow the instruction of the client because his opinion is very important
Try to follow the client’s request but make subtle changes to make the design look different
Try to convince the client to change design even with the risk of losing him
Ignore the client and proceed with your design

answer: C. Try to convince the client to change design even with the risk of losing him.
keyMemory: Kapag unethical ang request ng client, explain and persuade. The architect must protect professional integrity, even if it risks losing the commission.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Try to convince the client to change design even with the risk of losing him.
Ulitin natin: Try to convince the client to change the design, even with the risk of losing him.
Ito ang professional answer. Hindi porke client ang nagbabayad, susundin mo na lahat. Ang architect is not just a drafting hand. The architect is a professional adviser with ethical duties.
Kung pinapagawa ng client ay unethical, like copying another architect’s design, you must explain calmly but firmly. Sabihin mo na puwede nating kunin ang functional requirements, aesthetic preference, or design direction, pero kailangan original ang final design.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. Follow the instruction — mali. Client opinion matters, pero hindi above ethics and law.
b. Make subtle changes — trap ito. Parang “konting tweak lang para hindi halata.” Still unethical if the intent is to copy.
d. Ignore the client and proceed with your design — hindi rin professional. Kailangan mo siyang kausapin and educate, not just ignore.
c. Convince the client to change design — tama. Ito ang ethical, professional, and mature response.
Elimination method:
Kapag client asks you to copy, tanggal lahat ng may element ng copying. Tanggal A and B. Between C and D, mas professional ang C kasi may communication and persuasion. Hindi mo ini-ignore ang client.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag unethical ang request ng client, explain and persuade. The architect must protect professional integrity, even if it risks losing the commission.
________________________________________
visualReviewNote: Professional Practice subject: no images needed. Text/audio-ready explanation only.

question: A newspaper editor approaches an architect for an interview. Is it okay to ask to be paid for the interview?
answer: Yes, it is okay, if it is a proper professional interview or contribution and not a disguised advertisement.
keyMemory: An architect may be paid for legitimate professional contribution, like interviews or articles, but must avoid paid endorsements or disguised advertisements.
explanation:
Correct answer: Yes, it is okay, if it is a proper professional interview or contribution and not a disguised advertisement.
Ulitin natin: Yes, an architect may receive remuneration or honorarium for a proper professional interview, article, seminar, forum, or similar professional contribution.
Bakit okay? Kasi the Code allows the architect to participate in public knowledge activities. Puwede siyang magsulat, magbigay ng interview, mag-contribute sa publication, or mag-participate sa seminar or forum. Kung professional contribution ito, he may receive an honorarium or remuneration.
Pero may boundary: hindi ito dapat maging paid advertising disguised as professional content. Ibang usapan kapag ang architect ay ginagamit ang article para mag-promote ng product, material, contractor, or supplier. Iyon ang bawal.
So ang important distinction:
Interview for public knowledge? Okay.
Paid product endorsement or promotional advertisement? Not okay.
Elimination method dito kahit walang choices:
Tanungin mo sarili mo: “Is this public education or product promotion?”
Kung public education or professional interview, allowed.
Kung endorsement or advertisement, bawal.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
An architect may be paid for legitimate professional contribution, like interviews or articles, but must avoid paid endorsements or disguised advertisements.
________________________________________
visualReviewNote: Professional Practice subject: no images needed. Text/audio-ready explanation only.

question: Is it okay to endorse a product?
answer: No, especially if it is a paid advertisement endorsing construction materials, building materials, or equipment.
keyMemory: Architects may specify products professionally, but they should not act as paid endorsers of building materials or equipment.
explanation:
Correct answer: No, especially if it is a paid advertisement endorsing construction materials, building materials, or equipment.
Ulitin natin: No, an architect should not endorse a product in a paid advertisement.
Bakit bawal? Kasi architects have professional influence. Kapag architect ka at nag-endorse ka ng product, people may believe that the product is professionally superior because of your title. That can create conflict of interest and can damage public trust.
Sa ethics, the architect must refrain from taking part in paid advertisements endorsing construction or building materials or equipment. Hindi ka dapat magmukhang salesman ng produkto habang dala mo ang authority ng profession.
Important nuance: Puwede kang mag-specify materials in a project based on quality, standards, performance, and suitability. Pero iba ang professional specification sa paid endorsement.
Bakit bawal ang paid endorsement?
Kasi baka hindi na objective ang professional judgment mo. Baka isipin ng public na binabayaran ka to recommend something, not because it is truly appropriate.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Architects may specify products professionally, but they should not act as paid endorsers of building materials or equipment.
________________________________________
visualReviewNote: Professional Practice subject: no images needed. Text/audio-ready explanation only.

question: Arch. X is celebrating the 25th year anniversary of his office. He wants to be featured in a magazine. To lessen the amount that will be spent on being published, with his consent, he allowed his friend who is a writer to solicit from advertisers under his name. Is there anything wrong? If there is, what/who is wrong in this situation?

choices.text: Nothing is wrong. This was done with everybody’s consent and nobody was coerced into doing anything.
The writer friend of Architect X

keyMemory: An architect must not solicit, or allow others to solicit, advertisements or support under his name for a publication featuring his work.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Architect X is liable.
Ulitin natin: Architect X is liable.
Bakit? Kasi the rule says the architect shall not solicit, nor permit others to solicit in his name, advertisements or support toward the cost of any publication presenting his work.
Dito mismo nangyari iyon. The magazine feature is about Arch. X and his office. To reduce the cost, pinayagan niya ang writer friend niya na manghingi ng advertisers under his name. So kahit hindi siya mismo ang nanghingi, he permitted it.
That word is important: permit. Hindi lang bawal yung ikaw mismo ang mag-solicit. Bawal din yung papayagan mo ang ibang tao na gamitin ang pangalan mo para manghingi ng advertisement support for publication of your work.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. Nothing is wrong — mali. Consent does not automatically make something ethical.
c. The writer friend — the writer may have done the act, but the ethical rule applies to the architect, and the architect allowed his name to be used.
d. The advertisers — hindi sila ang main professional actor here. They simply responded to solicitation.
b. Architect X is liable — tama, because he permitted solicitation under his name.
Elimination method:
Hanapin ang phrase: with his consent and under his name. That points directly to Architect X. Kahit friend ang gumawa, Architect X allowed it. Kaya B.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
An architect must not solicit, or allow others to solicit, advertisements or support under his name for a publication featuring his work.
________________________________________
visualReviewNote: Professional Practice subject: no images needed. Text/audio-ready explanation only.

question: After the magazine was issued, another Architect Y noticed that some of his works were being claimed by Architect X in the magazine articles. Architect X said that he only did it because he wanted his writer friend to get more advertisers. Is there something wrong with this? If there is, who is liable?

choices.text: Nothing since Architect X is willing to settle with Architect Y
Architect X is liable of fraud for claiming Architect Y’s works
Both Architect X for fraud and the Writer for not checking his source
The Publisher for not checking the veracity of Architect X’s claims

answer: B. Architect X is liable of fraud for claiming Architect Y’s works.
keyMemory: Never claim another architect’s work. Authorship and intellectual property are protected. In professional practice, false credit is a serious ethical violation.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Architect X is liable of fraud for claiming Architect Y’s works.
Ulitin natin: Architect X is liable for claiming Architect Y’s works.
Ito ay clear violation of professional ethics. Hindi puwedeng i-claim ng isang architect ang work ng ibang architect. That is misrepresentation, fraud, and appropriation of another architect’s intellectual property.
Ang excuse ni Architect X na “ginawa ko lang para makakuha ng advertisers ang writer friend ko” does not excuse the violation. Motive does not erase unethical conduct. Kung hindi sa kanya ang work, hindi niya dapat kinlaim.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. Nothing since willing to settle — mali. Willingness to settle does not erase the unethical act.
c. Both Architect X and the Writer — tempting ito, pero under the architect’s professional ethics, the main liable person is Architect X, because he claimed another architect’s work. The writer may have journalistic responsibility, pero the Code being tested focuses on the architect’s conduct.
d. Publisher — publisher may have editorial responsibility, pero again, the professional violation is by Architect X.
b. Architect X is liable — tama, because he misrepresented authorship and appropriated another architect’s work.
Elimination method:
Keyword: his works were being claimed by Architect X. Kapag claim ng work ng iba, ang direct violator is the person claiming it. Tanggal publisher and writer as main answer. Tanggal “nothing” dahil settlement does not remove liability. Naiiwan B.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Never claim another architect’s work. Authorship and intellectual property are protected. In professional practice, false credit is a serious ethical violation.
visualReviewNote: Professional Practice subject: no images needed. Text/audio-ready explanation only.

question: Where should Architect Y file a complaint?
keyMemory: Complaint against professional conduct of an architect: PRC / PRBoA, not mayor, not NBI, not automatically court.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. PRC.
Ulitin natin: PRC ang tamang sagot.
Dito, ang context galing sa previous page: si Architect X claimed the works of Architect Y. So professional misconduct ito. Hindi ito ordinary barangay dispute, hindi rin business permit issue, at hindi rin mayor’s office matter.
Kapag ang complaint ay laban sa isang architect dahil sa unethical practice, misrepresentation, fraud, or violation of professional conduct, ang proper venue is the PRC, through the Professional Regulatory Board of Architecture.
Bakit hindi RTC? Court ito. Puwede later kung civil damages or legal case, pero for professional discipline, PRC muna ang pinaka-direct.
Bakit hindi Mayor’s Office? Wala silang authority to discipline an architect’s professional license.
Bakit hindi NBI? NBI is for criminal investigation. Pero ang tinatanong dito ay professional complaint against an architect.
Elimination method:
Hanapin mo ang clue: Architect Y wants to complain against Architect X for claiming his works.
Professional violation ba? Yes.
Sino ang may power over architect’s license? PRC.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Complaint against professional conduct of an architect: PRC / PRBoA, not mayor, not NBI, not automatically court.
________________________________________
visualReviewNote: Professional Practice subject: no images needed. Text/audio-ready explanation only.

question: What will happen to Architect X, if found guilty?

choices.text: His name will be deleted from the list of Registered Architects
Revocation of his Certificate of Registration

keyMemory: For unethical professional conduct, PRC/Board can discipline the architect. Usually, think suspension, and in grave cases, revocation of COR/PIC.
explanation:
Best answer: b. Suspension of License.
Ulitin natin: Suspension of License.
May konting nuance ito. Under RA 9266, kapag may violation such as unethical conduct, misrepresentation, or violation of the Act and IRR, the Board may impose disciplinary action, including suspension or revocation of the Certificate of Registration or Professional Identification Card.
Pero sa item na ito, dahil professional misconduct ang context — claiming another architect’s works — ang most reasonable board-answer among the choices is Suspension of License. Hindi automatic na revocation agad, unless grave ang finding or repeated/severe ang violation.
Bakit mali or weaker ang iba?
a. Revocation of Registration and License — possible in severe cases, pero too final and too broad for the situation as stated.
c. His name will be deleted from the list of Registered Architects — hindi ito ang normal phrasing ng disciplinary sanction.
d. Revocation of his Certificate of Registration — legally possible, pero mas severe. Kung ang question does not say “grave offense” or “after due hearing with revocation,” mas safe ang suspension.
Important: kung ang printed answer key says revocation of COR, hindi siya totally absurd, kasi RA 9266 allows revocation. Pero for this specific question, suspension is the cleaner answer unless the question explicitly asks for the heaviest penalty.
Elimination method:
Kapag “found guilty” sa professional misconduct, isipin mo muna: disciplinary sanction by PRC.
Tanggal “Mayor/NBI/court style penalties.”
Sa choices, the normal PRC professional sanction is suspension of license, with possible revocation depending on gravity.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
For unethical professional conduct, PRC/Board can discipline the architect. Usually, think suspension, and in grave cases, revocation of COR/PIC.
________________________________________
visualReviewNote: Professional Practice subject: no images needed. Text/audio-ready explanation only.

question: Is it ethical and legal for an architect to hire employees on a per-project basis since SSS and Medicare is not required but only group insurance and employees can be terminated even after 6 months?
answer: No, it is not ethical and not proper if the purpose is to avoid legal employee benefits and fair employment obligations.
keyMemory: Project-based work can be valid only if done lawfully and fairly. But using it to avoid benefits and employee obligations is unethical.
explanation:
Correct answer: No, it is not ethical and not proper if the purpose is to avoid legal employee benefits and fair employment obligations.
Ulitin natin: No, hindi ethical kung ang purpose ay iwasan ang SSS, Medicare/PhilHealth, employee benefits, and fair employment duties.
Ito ang point ng Code of Ethical Conduct in relation to colleagues and subordinates. The architect must provide employees and subordinates with a suitable work environment, fair compensation, and proper professional treatment.
Kung ginagamit ang “per project basis” para makaiwas sa benefits, security, and lawful treatment, hindi professional iyon. Hindi porke project-based ang arrangement ay automatic unethical. May legitimate project-based employment naman. Pero kung ang intent ay: “para hindi na mag-SSS, hindi na mag-Medicare/PhilHealth, at madaling tanggalin,” doon nagiging mali.
Bakit? Kasi ginagamit mo ang employment setup to avoid responsibility. As an architect-employer, may duty ka hindi lang sa client, kundi pati sa people under you.
Elimination method:
Keyword: SSS and Medicare not required and terminated even after 6 months.
Kapag ang framing ng question ay parang “paano makakaiwas sa employee obligations,” red flag iyon.
Kaya ang sagot: No, unethical and improper.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Project-based work can be valid only if done lawfully and fairly. But using it to avoid benefits and employee obligations is unethical.

question: What shall the architect not ask from sources such as material suppliers given the fact that he already receives a professional fee from the client?
answer: The architect must not ask for commissions, discounts, fees, gifts, favors, or kickbacks from suppliers, manufacturers, dealers, or agents.
keyMemory: Architect’s judgment must remain independent. No secret commissions, gifts, favors, or supplier kickbacks.
explanation:
Correct answer: The architect must not ask for commissions, discounts, fees, gifts, favors, or kickbacks from suppliers, manufacturers, dealers, or agents.
Ulitin natin: No commissions, no gifts, no favors, no kickbacks.
Ito ang ethics rule in relation to manufacturers, dealers, and agents. Kapag architect ka, dapat objective ang recommendation mo ng materials and equipment. Hindi ka dapat tumatanggap ng secret benefit from suppliers.
Bakit? Kasi binabayaran ka na ng client for your professional judgment. Kung tumanggap ka pa ng commission from supplier, magkakaroon ng conflict of interest. Baka hindi mo na pinipili ang best material for the client; baka pinipili mo na ang may kickback.
Important pa: kung may legitimate market discount, dapat iyon ay credited to the client, not secretly kept by the architect.
Bakit ito board exam favorite? Kasi gusto nilang makita kung naiintindihan mo na ang architect is a trusted professional, not a commission agent.
Elimination method:
Kapag may supplier, material dealer, manufacturer, agent, tapos may professional fee from client, automatic red flag ang: commission, gift, discount, favor, or kickback.
Lahat ng hidden benefit, tanggal dapat.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Architect’s judgment must remain independent. No secret commissions, gifts, favors, or supplier kickbacks.

question: How much will an Architect pay if he/she violates the Code of Ethical Conduct?
answer: A. ₱100,000.00 — but remember the full penalty range is ₱100,000 to ₱5,000,000.
keyMemory: Penalty fine range: ₱100,000 minimum to ₱5,000,000 maximum. Huwag kalimutan ang range, kahit ang MCQ answer dito ay ₱100,000.
explanation:
Best answer: a. ₱100,000.00 — but remember the full penalty range is ₱100,000 to ₱5,000,000.
Ulitin natin: minimum fine: ₱100,000.00.
Ito medyo tricky kasi ang law provision says the fine is not less than ₱100,000 and not more than ₱5,000,000, or imprisonment, or both, depending on the court.
So bakit ₱100,000 ang piliin? Kasi kung single amount lang ang hinahanap at hindi tinanong kung maximum, ang board-exam answer usually points to the minimum fine, meaning not less than ₱100,000.
Bakit hindi ₱5,000,000?
₱5,000,000 is the maximum. Kung ang question sana ay “maximum fine,” then that would be the answer. Pero dito, general “how much will pay” and choices include the minimum; kaya safer ang ₱100,000.
Bakit hindi ₱150,000 or ₱200,000?
Wala silang special legal basis sa provision. Random distractors sila.
Elimination method:
Read the legal phrase: not less than ₱100,000 but not more than ₱5,000,000.
Kapag tinanong minimum: ₱100,000.
Kapag tinanong maximum: ₱5,000,000.
Kapag vague ang question, sa ALE usually piliin ang minimum stated fine: ₱100,000.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Penalty fine range: ₱100,000 minimum to ₱5,000,000 maximum. Huwag kalimutan ang range, kahit ang MCQ answer dito ay ₱100,000.

question: Archt. Steve explains his methodology to the selection committee regarding how he would translate the plan/design requirements of the proposed store renovation of CMG store in Rockwell. Under what stage in the comparative selection process is this?
keyMemory: Kapag architect is personally explaining approach, methodology, and design process to a selection committee, that is the Interview stage.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Interview.
Ulitin natin: Interview.
Ang clue dito ay explains his methodology to the selection committee. Kapag ang architect ay ine-explain kung paano niya ia-approach ang project, paano niya i-ta-translate ang design requirements, at paano niya gagawin ang work, that is part of the interview stage.
Bakit hindi Evaluation and Ranking?
Evaluation and ranking is where the committee compares qualifications, experience, and submissions. Hindi necessarily doon personal na ine-explain ng architect ang methodology.
Bakit hindi Negotiation?
Negotiation comes after selection or shortlisting. Dito pinag-uusapan na ang terms, scope, fee, and agreement details.
Bakit hindi Verification?
Verification is checking information, credentials, or references. Hindi ito methodology presentation.
Elimination method:
Keyword: explains methodology to the selection committee.
Presentation and explanation = Interview.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag architect is personally explaining approach, methodology, and design process to a selection committee, that is the Interview stage.

question: In what phase of the architectural design services does the feasibility study is conducted?
keyMemory: Feasibility study happens before design proper, kaya under Pre-Design Services.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Pre-Design Services.
Ulitin natin: Feasibility study is under Pre-Design Services.
Bakit? Kasi feasibility study happens before full design. Dito pa lang tinitingnan kung viable ba ang project: practical ba, financially possible ba, suitable ba ang site, ano ang constraints, ano ang risks, and ano ang probable direction.
Regular Design Services start when the architect proceeds with schematic design, design development, contract documents, and related design phases. Pero feasibility is earlier. It helps the client decide whether to proceed.
Bakit mali ang iba?
b. Regular Design Services — ito na ang normal design process, after pre-design decisions.
c. Specialized Architectural Services — these are special services beyond regular scope.
d. Comprehensive Architectural Services — broader package ito, but feasibility as a specific item is classically pre-design.
Elimination method:
Keyword: feasibility study.
Kapag tinatanong kung feasible pa lang, ibig sabihin project is not yet fully designed. So sagot: Pre-Design Services.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Feasibility study happens before design proper, kaya under Pre-Design Services.

question: In what type of services do making of brochures/leaflets/fliers/posters and promotional & advertisement campaign are included?
keyMemory: Feasibility studies and promotional services are both under Pre-Design Services in this reviewer’s classification.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Pre-Design Services.
Ulitin natin: Promotional materials and promotional campaigns are under Pre-Design Services.
Medyo surprising ito, kasi brochures and flyers sound like marketing, not architecture. Pero in the Standards of Professional Practice, promotional services can be part of the pre-design package. This may include preparation of brochures, leaflets, fliers, posters, and promotional or advertisement campaigns for a project.
Bakit siya pre-design? Kasi before full design or before project launching, the architect may help communicate the project concept, positioning, and presentation materials. Lalo na sa developments, sales campaigns, or proposed projects, promotional materials can be prepared before or alongside early project planning.
Bakit hindi Regular Design Services?
Regular design is more about schematic design, design development, contract documents, and construction-related design phases.
Bakit hindi Specialized Architectural Services?
May visual and presentation services na specialized, yes, pero this exact wording — brochures, fliers, posters, promotional campaigns — is commonly classified under Promotional Services, which falls under Pre-Design Services in this reviewer context.
Bakit hindi Comprehensive Architectural Services?
Too broad. The question asks a specific service type.
Elimination method:
Keyword: brochures, leaflets, fliers, posters, promotional and advertisement campaign.
Memory hook: Promotional Services = Pre-Design Services.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Feasibility studies and promotional services are both under Pre-Design Services in this reviewer’s classification.

question: An Architect drew up plans for a family but then the owners went abroad. After a year the owners returned but instructed the architect to help them find a new lot. The task would entail the architect’s personal time. What stage in the Architect’s services will the Architect perform?
keyMemory: Kapag ang task ay site selection, lot evaluation, feasibility, or programming, nasa Pre-Design Services ka pa.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Pre-Design Services.
Ulitin natin: Pre-Design Services.
Bakit Pre-Design? Kasi ang pinapagawa sa architect ay help find a new lot. Hindi pa ito design proper. Hindi pa ito schematic design, design development, or working drawings. Nasa stage pa lang sila na hinahanap at sinusuri ang project site.
Sa SPP classification, kasama sa Pre-Design Services ang site selection and analysis, site evaluation, consultation, feasibility studies, site utilization, land use studies, programming, and other early studies bago magsimula ang actual design.
Kaya kahit “personal time” ang ginamit sa question, huwag kang madadala. Ang tunay na clue ay find a new lot. Kapag lot selection, site search, site evaluation, feasibility, or programming, ang sagot usually Pre-Design Services.
Bakit mali ang iba?
b. Regular Design Services — ito na ang normal design phases like schematic design, design development, contract documents, bidding, and construction phase services. Pero dito, wala pa tayo sa design proper.
c. Specialized Architectural Services — ito ay mga special services beyond the normal scope, like detailed specialty studies, environmental planning, acoustic design, or other specialized consultancy. Pero finding and evaluating a lot is commonly pre-design.
d. Comprehensive Architectural Services — broader package ito, parang full-service engagement. Pero ang specific task na tinatanong ay lot finding or site selection, so mas exact ang Pre-Design Services.
Elimination method:
Tanungin mo: “Nagde-design na ba siya ng building?” Hindi pa.
“Gumagawa na ba siya ng working drawings?” Hindi.
“Site or lot selection ba ito?” Yes.
Kaya Pre-Design Services.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag ang task ay site selection, lot evaluation, feasibility, or programming, nasa Pre-Design Services ka pa.

question: What Architectural Service/Phase is the part where the Architect and Client choose the Contractor?
keyMemory: Contractor selection happens in the Bidding Phase, not during design and not during construction.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. Bidding Phase.
Ulitin natin: Bidding Phase.
Ito ang phase kung saan tinutulungan ng architect ang owner or client na makakuha ng bids or proposals from contractors, mag-review ng bid documents, mag-compare ng submissions, and eventually recommend or assist in selecting the contractor.
Ang keyword dito ay choose the Contractor. Hindi pa ito construction, kasi hindi pa nagsisimula ang actual work. Hindi na rin ito contract document phase, kasi tapos na dapat ang drawings and specifications na gagamitin for bidding. Ang pagpili ng contractor happens during the Bidding Phase.
Bakit mali ang iba?
a. Contract Document — ito ang preparation ng working drawings, specifications, and documents. Ginagamit ito para makapag-bidding, pero hindi dito mismo pinipili ang contractor.
b. Evaluation Phase — may evaluation of bids, yes, pero as a formal architectural service phase, mas tamang umbrella term ay Bidding Phase.
c. Construction Phase — dito nagsisimula na ang actual construction after contractor selection and contract award. Too late na ito.
d. Bidding Phase — tama. Dito pumapasok ang contractor selection.
Elimination method:
Kapag ang tanong ay sino ang contractor na kukunin, isipin mo: bidding, bid evaluation, recommendation, award. Sa choices, ang official phase is Bidding Phase.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Contractor selection happens in the Bidding Phase, not during design and not during construction.

visualReviewNote: Professional Practice subject: no images needed. Text/audio-ready explanation only.

question: Preparation of working drawings, specifications and other documents and submission to client, what stage is this?
keyMemory: Kapag working drawings, technical specifications, and documents for construction ang usapan, sagot: Contract Document Phase.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Contract Document Phase.
Ulitin natin: Contract Document Phase.
Ito ang stage kung saan ginagawa ang complete drawings and documents needed for bidding, permits, and construction. Kasama rito ang working drawings, technical specifications, schedules, and other contract documents.
Ang clue dito ay working drawings. Kapag narinig mo ang working drawings, usually tapos na ang schematic and design development. Nasa detailed documentation stage ka na. Ito ang magiging basis ng contractor for pricing and construction.
Bakit mali ang iba?
b. Construction Phase — dito ginagamit ang documents, pero hindi dito primarily pinaprepare ang working drawings.
c. Design Development Phase — dito nire-refine ang design, plans, elevations, sections, systems, and outline specifications. Pero hindi pa ito final working drawings package.
d. Bidding Phase — dito ginagamit ang contract documents to invite bids. Pero the preparation of the documents happens before bidding.
Elimination method:
Keyword: working drawings and specifications.
Working drawings equal final detailed documentation.
Final detailed documentation equal Contract Document Phase.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag working drawings, technical specifications, and documents for construction ang usapan, sagot: Contract Document Phase.

visualReviewNote: Professional Practice subject: no images needed. Text/audio-ready explanation only.

question: At what stage are specifications done?
keyMemory: Final specifications are completed in the Contract Document Phase. Outline specifications appear earlier in Design Development, pero final specs belong to contract documents.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Contract Document Phase.
Ulitin natin: Contract Document Phase.
Important distinction ito. Sa Design Development Phase, may outline specifications na. Ibig sabihin, early description pa lang ng materials, systems, finishes, and general quality level. Pero kapag tinanong na specifications are done, ang tinutukoy usually ay final or technical specifications, and those are completed in the Contract Document Phase.
So ang board exam logic:
Outline specifications — Design Development Phase.
Final technical specifications — Contract Document Phase.
Bakit mali ang iba?
b. Construction Phase — specs are used during construction, pero dapat tapos na sila bago construction.
c. Design Development Phase — may outline specs dito, pero not the final complete specifications.
d. Specification Phase — trap ito. Walang separate official “Specification Phase” sa standard regular design services.
Elimination method:
Kapag may choice na Specification Phase, ingat ka. Usually invented choice iyan. Specs are part of the contract documents. Kaya ang best answer is Contract Document Phase.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Final specifications are completed in the Contract Document Phase. Outline specifications appear earlier in Design Development, pero final specs belong to contract documents.

question: Stage where Architect makes decisions on all claims of the Owner and Contractors on all matters relating to the execution and progress or the interpretation of the Contract Documents?
keyMemory: Claims, progress issues, site execution concerns, and interpretation of contract documents are handled during the Construction Phase.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Construction Phase.
Ulitin natin: Construction Phase.
Bakit Construction Phase? Kasi ang question says execution and progress of the work and interpretation of the Contract Documents. Nangyayari ito habang ginagawa na ang project. During construction, the architect helps administer the contract, checks work progress, interprets drawings and specifications, and may make decisions on claims or disputes related to the contract documents.
Hindi ibig sabihin na judge siya sa court. Ang role niya ay professional interpreter and administrator of the construction contract, acting fairly between owner and contractor.
Bakit mali ang iba?
a. Contract Document Phase — dito ginagawa ang documents, pero wala pang construction claims and progress issues.
c. Design Development Phase — design refinement stage ito. Wala pang contractor claims or execution issues.
d. Specification Phase — again, trap ito. Hindi official phase, and hindi ito ang stage for deciding owner-contractor claims.
b. Construction Phase — tama, kasi actual execution, progress, interpretation, and claims happen during construction.
Elimination method:
Keyword: execution and progress.
Kapag may execution, progress, claims, contractor, and interpretation of contract documents, nasa Construction Phase ka.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Claims, progress issues, site execution concerns, and interpretation of contract documents are handled during the Construction Phase.

question: Under what type of service is “sound insulation treatment”?
keyMemory: Sound insulation treatment is not just ordinary design detailing. It is part of Acoustic Design, under Specialized Architectural Services.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. Specialized Arch’l.
Ulitin natin: sound insulation treatment is under Specialized Architectural Services.
Bakit? Kasi ang sound insulation, sound absorption, sound reflection, echo control, noise control, and acoustic treatment are under Acoustic Design Services. At ang Acoustic Design Services ay part ng Specialized Architectural Services.
Hindi ito normal regular design lang. Sa regular design, ginagawa ng architect ang schematic design, design development, contract documents, bidding, and construction phase services. Pero kapag specific technical concern na tulad ng acoustics, soundproofing, reverberation, noise transmission, and acoustic performance, specialized na iyon.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. Pre-Design — mali. Pre-design is for feasibility study, site selection, programming, consultations, and early studies. Hindi doon ang detailed sound insulation treatment.
b. Comprehensive Arch’l — too broad. Comprehensive services may include many services, pero ang exact classification ng sound insulation treatment is specialized.
c. Regular Design — regular design may show walls, ceilings, materials, and rooms, pero acoustic treatment itself is a specialized service.
d. Specialized Arch’l — tama, kasi sound insulation belongs to acoustic design services.
Elimination method:
Kapag narinig mo ang sound, noise, acoustics, insulation, or reverberation, isipin agad: Acoustic Design.
Acoustic Design equals Specialized Architectural Services.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sound insulation treatment is not just ordinary design detailing. It is part of Acoustic Design, under Specialized Architectural Services.

question: Where does physical planning services fall?
keyMemory: Physical planning is a specialized architectural service because it deals with the larger physical arrangement of land, spaces, and development.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. Specialized Arch’l.
Ulitin natin: Physical Planning Services fall under Specialized Architectural Services.
Physical planning is not just drawing a building. It deals with the broader arrangement of land, buildings, circulation, open spaces, utilities, development controls, and the physical organization of an area. Kaya siya mas malawak kaysa regular building design.
Sa SPP classification, Site and Physical Planning Services are considered Specialized Architectural Services. Kasama rito ang planning of sites, land uses, circulation, spatial relationships, and development layouts.
Bakit mali ang iba?
a. Pre-Design — may overlap sa early studies, pero the service classification asked here is physical planning services. That falls under specialized architectural services.
b. Design and Planning — vague ito. Hindi ito standard category sa choices.
c. Regular Design — regular design services focus on one project’s architectural design phases. Physical planning is broader and more specialized.
d. Specialized Arch’l — tama.
Elimination method:
Keyword: physical planning.
Kapag planning na lumalampas sa building design and deals with site or area development, hindi na siya regular design. Piliin ang Specialized Architectural Services.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Physical planning is a specialized architectural service because it deals with the larger physical arrangement of land, spaces, and development.

question: Owner A wants to build a Diplomatic Function Hall for at least 800 persons. He hires Architect B for the project. Architect B later informs Owner A that they will need to hire a Professional since he has limited knowledge in function hall designs. Who should they hire?
keyMemory: For large halls and assembly spaces, acoustics is a major design concern. The architect should coordinate with an Acoustics Engineer or Acoustic Designer when needed.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Acoustics Engineer.
Ulitin natin: they should hire an Acoustics Engineer.
Bakit? Function hall ito for at least 800 persons. Kapag malaking function hall, very important ang sound quality: speech intelligibility, echo control, reverberation, sound insulation, noise control, and acoustic treatment.
Yes, architect ang main designer. Pero kapag limited ang knowledge niya sa specialized acoustic requirements, professional and ethical na mag-recommend siya ng specialist. Hindi kahinaan iyon. Good practice iyon. Architect coordinates with consultants when the project needs specialized expertise.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
a. ECE — Electronics and Communications Engineer may help with sound systems, communication systems, audio equipment, and electronics. Pero ang issue here is function hall design and acoustic performance. Mas direct ang Acoustics Engineer.
c. Events Planner — events planner handles event logistics, program flow, setup, maybe guest movement. Hindi siya ang professional for architectural acoustic design.
d. Architect — may architect na nga, si Architect B. Ang kulang ay specialized acoustic expertise, not another general architect.
b. Acoustics Engineer — tama, because a function hall needs proper acoustic design.
Elimination method:
Keyword: function hall for 800 persons.
Kapag hall, auditorium, theater, lecture room, or function space, isipin: acoustics.
Tanggal events planner kasi event operations iyon. Tanggal ECE kung sound equipment lang, not acoustic space performance. Tanggal architect kasi may architect na. Naiiwan Acoustics Engineer.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
For large halls and assembly spaces, acoustics is a major design concern. The architect should coordinate with an Acoustics Engineer or Acoustic Designer when needed.

question: For specialized architectural services, what is the activity of total planning that encompasses social, physical and environmental factors?
keyMemory: Comprehensive Development Planning is bigger than site planning. It looks at the full development picture: people, land, environment, infrastructure, and future growth.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Comprehensive Development Planning.
Ulitin natin: Comprehensive Development Planning.
Bakit ito ang tama? Kasi ang clue ay total planning and it includes social, physical, and environmental factors. Kapag hindi lang building or site layout ang pinag-uusapan, kundi mas malawak na development direction involving people, land, environment, infrastructure, and long-term growth, that is Comprehensive Development Planning.
Physical planning focuses mainly on the physical arrangement of land and facilities. Environmental planning focuses heavily on environmental systems and impacts. Pero kapag pinagsama ang social, physical, environmental, and development aspects, mas complete ang Comprehensive Development Planning.

a. Physical Planning — too narrow. Physical arrangement lang mainly ang focus.
c. Environmental Planning — important, pero environmental side lang ang emphasis. Hindi nito sakop lahat ng social and physical planning in the same broad way.
d. Comprehensive Arch’l Services — broad service package ito, pero hindi ito ang specific activity described by the question.
b. Comprehensive Development Planning — tama, because it is total planning.
Elimination method:
Keyword: total planning.
Kapag total planning plus social, physical, and environmental factors, piliin ang Comprehensive Development Planning.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Comprehensive Development Planning is bigger than site planning. It looks at the full development picture: people, land, environment, infrastructure, and future growth.

question: For specialized architectural services, what is the aesthetic designing of the space of a structure?
keyMemory: Space planning is about functional arrangement. Architectural Interiors is about the designed interior environment, including aesthetic treatment.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Architectural Interiors.
Ulitin natin: Architectural Interiors.
Bakit? Ang question says aesthetic designing of the space of a structure. That points to the interior environment: spatial character, finishes, built-ins, furniture coordination, lighting mood, color, texture, and overall interior design expression. In architectural services, this is Architectural Interiors.
Bakit hindi space planning? Space planning is more about functional layout: relationships of rooms, circulation, furniture arrangement, and efficiency of space. Pero kapag sinabi na aesthetic designing, interior design character na iyon.
Bakit mali ang iba?
a. Site Development Planning — exterior/site planning ito, not interior space aesthetics.
b. Space Planning Services — close, pero more functional and organizational, not primarily aesthetic.
d. Architectural Programming Services — this is about identifying user needs, space requirements, functions, and project criteria before design.
c. Architectural Interiors — tama, because it covers the aesthetic and functional treatment of interior spaces.
Elimination method:
Keyword: aesthetic designing of the space.
Kapag aesthetic interior space, hindi site, hindi programming. Between space planning and interiors, mas exact ang Architectural Interiors.

Space planning is about functional arrangement. Architectural Interiors is about the designed interior environment, including aesthetic treatment.

question: You were commissioned to do a Master Plan. What should you do first?

choices.text: Coordinate with the local government for tracing of history

keyMemory: In master planning, do not draw first. Know the laws, zoning, ordinances, and development controls first.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Study the existing laws.
Ulitin natin: Study the existing laws first.
Bakit? Sa master planning, hindi ka pwedeng basta mag-layout ng roads, buildings, open spaces, and land uses based only on preference. Kailangan mo munang malaman ang legal and planning framework: zoning ordinance, land use plan, development controls, easements, environmental restrictions, building laws, local ordinances, and government requirements.
Yes, in actual practice, magse-site survey ka rin. Important iyon. Pero sa board exam logic, kapag tinatanong ang first professional move in master planning, the safest answer is to understand the existing laws and development controls first. Kasi sila ang magdidikta kung ano ang allowed, restricted, or prohibited.
Bakit mali ang iba?
b. Survey the location — very important, pero kung hindi mo alam ang legal controls, baka mali ang assumptions mo sa master plan. Site survey and legal study often happen together, but the board-style best answer here is existing laws.
c. Coordinate with LGU for tracing of history — helpful for heritage or local context, pero hindi ito first general step for all master plans.
d. Direct all department heads — management action ito, hindi planning analysis step.
Elimination method:
Keyword: Master Plan.
Master plan must comply with zoning, land use, laws, and development controls. Kaya first, study existing laws. Survey follows or happens alongside it, pero the controlling framework is legal.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
In master planning, do not draw first. Know the laws, zoning, ordinances, and development controls first.

question: Who does the architect approach for sound control?

Bakit mali ang iba?
a. Electronics Engineer — may role sa electronic sound systems, pero not the main answer for architectural sound control.
c. Electrical Engineer — handles electrical power, lighting circuits, and related electrical systems. Hindi siya primary acoustic specialist.
d. DJ — obviously not a design consultant for building acoustics. Pang-event/music operation siya, not architectural sound control.
b. Acoustic Designer — tama.
Elimination method:
Keyword: sound control.
Kapag space acoustics ang problem, piliin Acoustic Designer, not DJ, not general electrical/electronics.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sound system is different from sound control. For sound control in buildings, go to an Acoustic Designer.

question: What is considered NOT part of Specialized Architectural Services?
keyMemory: Sa “NOT part” questions, huwag piliin ang familiar specialized terms. Hanapin ang hindi standard sa list. Dito, ang outlier ay Equipment Evaluation.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Equipment Evaluation.
Ulitin natin: Equipment Evaluation is NOT part of Specialized Architectural Services.
Sa SPP classification, Forensic Architecture, Value Management, and Building Appraisal are recognized specialized architectural services. They go beyond regular design services and require particular expertise.
Equipment Evaluation, as written, is not one of the standard named specialized architectural services in this context. May mga services na may relation to building systems, facilities, testing, commissioning, or security evaluation, pero Equipment Evaluation as a standalone item is not the listed service being tested here.
Bakit mali ang ibang choices?
b. Forensic Architecture — part ito ng specialized services. It deals with investigation, defects, failures, disputes, and professional findings related to buildings.
c. Value Management — part of specialized services. It focuses on optimizing value, cost, performance, and project decisions.
d. Building Appraisal — part of specialized services. It involves professional assessment of building value, condition, or related appraisal concerns.
a. Equipment Evaluation — ito ang hindi kasama sa standard list, kaya ito ang sagot.
Elimination method:
Kapag “NOT part” ang question, hanapin ang outlier.
Forensic Architecture? Recognized.
Value Management? Recognized.
Building Appraisal? Recognized.
Equipment Evaluation? Hindi standard named specialized architectural service dito. Kaya A.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Sa “NOT part” questions, huwag piliin ang familiar specialized terms. Hanapin ang hindi standard sa list. Dito, ang outlier ay Equipment Evaluation.

question: If an Architect assumes the function of going to the project site for monitoring and supervision, what service is being provided?
keyMemory: Site monitoring and supervision as a service = Full-time Construction Supervision.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. Full-time Construction Supervision.
Ulitin natin: Full-time Construction Supervision.
Ang clue dito ay going to the project site for monitoring and supervision. Kapag ang architect or his representative is assigned to monitor the site, inspect work, evaluate construction progress, prepare daily inspection reports, and check if the work is being done according to plans and specifications, that points to Full-time Construction Supervision.
Huwag malito sa Construction Management. Construction Management is broader. It includes coordination, time control, cost control, quality control, and record keeping. Pero kung ang wording ay site monitoring and supervision, mas direct ang sagot na Full-time Construction Supervision.
Bakit mali ang iba?
a. Construction Inspector — ito ay person or role, hindi service classification.
b. Construction Management — broader management service, hindi lang site monitoring.
c. Project Supervision — sounds close, pero hindi ito ang proper SPP service title sa choices.
d. Full-time Construction Supervision — pinaka-exact.
Elimination method:
Kapag ang focus ay site visits, monitoring, supervision, inspection reports, piliin mo ang Full-time Construction Supervision. Kapag ang focus ay coordination, time, cost, quality, records, doon mas papasok ang Construction Management.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Site monitoring and supervision as a service = Full-time Construction Supervision.

question: What do you call the person or group appointed by the owner after the completion of the building?

explanation:
Correct answer: d. Building Administrator.
Ulitin natin: Building Administrator.

Ang kailangan na ay someone or a group to handle the day-to-day operation, maintenance, tenant concerns, utilities, security coordination, and general management of the completed facility.
That is the Building Administrator.
Bakit mali ang iba?
a. Construction Manager — habang construction ito, not after completion.
b. Project Consultant — too broad and vague.
c. Project Supervisor — also related more to project execution.
d. Building Administrator — tama, kasi post-completion operation and administration na ito.
Elimination method:
Keyword: after completion. Construction-related titles become weaker. Building operation title becomes stronger. Kaya Building Administrator.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
After construction, the person managing the completed building is the Building Administrator.

question: What is the last phase of an architectural service by administration?
keyMemory: The last stage after construction is Post Construction Service.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. Post Construction Service.
Ulitin natin: Post Construction Service.
Ang word na last phase ang clue. After pre-design, regular design, bidding, construction, and other delivery services, ang huling phase is what happens after construction: building administration, maintenance support, post-construction evaluation, and checking the building after occupancy or use.
Kaya ang sagot ay Post Construction Service.
Bakit mali ang iba?
a. Construction Management — still during construction. Hindi last after completion.
b. Project Management — can span many phases, but not specifically the last phase.
c. Design-built by administration — project delivery method, not the last phase.
d. Post Construction Service — tama, because it comes after construction.
Elimination method:
Kapag may word na last phase, hanapin mo yung may post. Post means after. Kaya Post Construction Service.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
The last stage after construction is Post Construction Service.

question: Other function of an architect?
keyMemory: Architects may serve as Project Managers, but should be careful with roles like supplier, manufacturer, or fabricator because of conflict-of-interest issues.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Project Manager.
Ulitin natin: Project Manager.
An architect may perform other professional functions related to managing the project: coordinating consultants, schedules, documentation, client requirements, project direction, and sometimes administration of project delivery.
Pero hindi siya normally acting as a fabricator, manufacturer, or supplier in the ethical-professional sense. Those roles can create conflict of interest, especially if the architect is also specifying materials or evaluating work.
Bakit mali ang iba?
b. Fabricator — product-making role ito, not the normal professional function of an architect.
c. Manufacturer — producer of goods, not architect’s professional function.
d. Supplier — supplier of materials, possible conflict of interest.
a. Project Manager — tama, related to professional architectural/project services.
Elimination method:
Hanapin mo kung alin ang professional management role. Project Manager lang ang connected sa architect’s expanded professional service. The other three are commercial supply/production roles.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Architects may serve as Project Managers, but should be careful with roles like supplier, manufacturer, or fabricator because of conflict-of-interest issues.

question: It is the most common method of compensation which is also fair to both client and architect?
keyMemory: Most common compensation method in this context: Percentage Fee Method.
explanation:
Correct answer: c. Percentage Fee Method.
Ulitin natin: Percentage Fee Method.
Ito ang common method where the architect’s fee is computed as a percentage of the project construction cost. It is commonly used because it relates the architect’s compensation to the scale and complexity of the project.
Bakit siya considered fair? Kasi habang lumalaki ang project cost and responsibility, tumataas din ang corresponding professional fee. Hindi siya perfect in all cases, pero sa board exam context, ito ang standard answer for most common method.
Bakit mali ang iba?
a. Multiple of Direct Personnel Expense — useful kapag uncertain ang scope or time involved, but not the most common general method.
b. Professional Fee plus Expenses — possible compensation arrangement, but not the classic “most common” answer.
d. Lump Sum — fixed fee ito; useful kapag malinaw ang scope, pero hindi siya generally described as the most common and fair in this reviewer context.
c. Percentage Fee Method — tama.
Elimination method:
Keyword: most common method. Sa compensation ng architect, board memory: Percentage Fee Method.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Most common compensation method in this context: Percentage Fee Method.

question: If the Owner continuously orders changes to the design, what methods of compensation should be used?
keyMemory: Kapag paulit-ulit ang client changes and hindi na predictable ang scope, use Per Diem or MDPE compensation.
explanation:
Correct answer: d. Per Diem and MDPE.
Ulitin natin: Per Diem and MDPE.
Kapag paulit-ulit ang changes, hindi na stable ang scope of work. Mahirap i-price using lump sum kasi hindi mo alam ilang revisions pa ang ipapagawa. Kaya mas fair gumamit ng time-based or personnel-based compensation.
Per Diem means payment based on time spent, usually per day or specific time period.
MDPE, or Multiple of Direct Personnel Expense, means compensation based on the direct personnel cost multiplied by a factor to cover overhead and profit.
Bakit mali ang iba?
a. Lump Sum — risky kapag pabago-bago ang scope. Fixed fee ito, so lugi ang architect if revisions keep increasing.
b. MDPE and Percentage Fee — may MDPE, pero percentage fee is not the cleanest method for continuous revisions.
c. Percentage Fee and Cost Method — less direct for repeated design changes.
d. Per Diem and MDPE — tama, because changes are uncertain and time/personnel effort must be compensated.
Elimination method:
Keyword: continuously orders changes. Kapag unstable ang scope, avoid fixed fee. Choose time/personnel-based method: Per Diem and MDPE.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Kapag paulit-ulit ang client changes and hindi na predictable ang scope, use Per Diem or MDPE compensation.

question: An Architect was commissioned for a project amounting to ₱3,500,000.00 including 12% VAT. How much would it be without VAT?
keyMemory: VAT-inclusive amount: divide by 1.12 to remove VAT.
explanation:
Correct answer: ₱3,125,000.00.
Ulitin natin: without VAT, the amount is ₱3,125,000.00.
Dahil ang ₱3,500,000 ay VAT-inclusive, hindi natin basta ibabawas ang 12% of ₱3,500,000. Ang tamang move is divide by 1.12.
Formula:
₱3,500,000 ÷ 1.12 = ₱3,125,000
Yung VAT portion naman is:
₱3,500,000 − ₱3,125,000 = ₱375,000
Bakit hindi ₱3,080,000? Kasi iyon ang lalabas kapag mali ang method mo: ₱3,500,000 minus 12% of ₱3,500,000. That is wrong for VAT-inclusive amounts.
Elimination method:
Kapag sinabi including VAT, divide by 1.12.
Kapag sinabi plus VAT, multiply by 1.12.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
VAT-inclusive amount: divide by 1.12 to remove VAT.
________________________________________
visualReviewNote: Professional Practice subject: no images needed. Text/audio-ready explanation only.

question: The fee is broken down as 30% overhead expenses, 40% salaries/fees expenses, and 30% profit. How much would be his net profit?
keyMemory: Compute profit from the VAT-exclusive amount, not from the VAT-inclusive billing.
explanation:
Correct answer: ₱937,500.00.
Ulitin natin: net profit is ₱937,500.00.
Use the VAT-exclusive amount, not the VAT-inclusive amount.
VAT-exclusive professional fee = ₱3,125,000
Profit percentage = 30%
So:
₱3,125,000 × 30% = ₱937,500
Bakit VAT-exclusive ang base? Kasi VAT is not income or profit of the architect. VAT is tax collected and remitted. Kaya kapag kinukuha ang overhead, salaries, and profit, ang proper base is the amount without VAT.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Compute profit from the VAT-exclusive amount, not from the VAT-inclusive billing.
________________________________________
visualReviewNote: Professional Practice subject: no images needed. Text/audio-ready explanation only.

question: How much would be his tax, WT, to the government? Best board-style answer: ₱312,500.00 if WT is taken as 10% of the VAT-exclusive professional fee. Ulitin natin: WT is commonly computed from the VAT-exclusive professional fee. VAT-exclusive professional fee = ₱3,125,000 Assumed withholding tax rate in this type of reviewer problem = 10% So: ₱3,125,000 × 10% = ₱312,500 Important note, ha. In real Philippine taxation, withholding tax can depend on the taxpayer type, income threshold, and current BIR rules. But for this reviewer-style computation, kapag sinabi lang WT and walang ibang rate given, the usual board-review assumption is 10% of the VAT-exclusive professional fee. May possible wrong shortcut: kunin ang 10% ng profit only, which gives ₱93,750. Pero withholding tax is normally based on the professional fee/payment, not merely the architect’s net profit. Kaya mas technically sound ang ₱312,500. Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito? For VAT-inclusive fee problems: First, remove VAT. Second, compute profit from VAT-exclusive amount. Third, if WT is assumed at 10%, apply it to the VAT-exclusive professional fee. ________________________________________ Quick recap of the computation Gross fee including VAT: ₱3,500,000 VAT-exclusive fee: ₱3,125,000 VAT portion: ₱375,000 Net profit at 30%: ₱937,500 WT at 10% of VAT-exclusive fee: ₱312,500 Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito? Kapag including VAT, divide by 1.12. Huwag basta ibawas ang 12% directly from the gross amount.

question: An Architect drew up plans for a family but then the owners went abroad. After a year, the owners returned but instructed the architect to help them find a new lot. The task would entail the architect’s personal time. What will be the ideal method of compensation that the Architect should use if he is to find a new site for the project?
answer: B. Per diem plus reimbursable expenses.
keyMemory: Site finding and personal-time services are best compensated by per diem plus reimbursable expenses.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Per diem plus reimbursable expenses.
Ulitin natin: Per diem plus reimbursable expenses.
Bakit ito ang tama? Kasi ang task dito is not yet regular building design. Ang pinapagawa ay help find a new site. That means site search, site visit, evaluation, travel, meetings, and personal time ng architect. Hindi ito madaling i-base sa construction cost kasi wala pa ngang final site and possibly wala pang updated project cost.
Kaya mas fair ang per diem plus reimbursable expenses. Per diem covers the architect’s time. Reimbursable expenses cover transportation, meals, lodging kung malayo, communication, printing, documents, and other actual costs related to the service.
Bakit mali ang iba?
a. Percentage of Construction Cost — hindi ideal dito kasi hindi pa design/construction cost-based ang task. Site finding ito, pre-design activity.
c. Multiple of Direct Personnel Expense — possible method ito for some pre-design services, especially kung maraming staff involved. Pero dito ang clue is personal time ng architect, kaya per diem ang mas direct.
d. Lump Sum or Fixed Fee — puwede kung clearly defined ang scope, pero sa site search, uncertain ang time. Hindi mo alam ilang site ang iche-check, ilang meetings, ilang travel. So mas safe ang per diem plus reimbursable.
Elimination method:
Keyword: personal time and find a new site.
Kapag personal time ang emphasis, huwag percentage. Huwag fixed fee kung uncertain. Piliin ang time-based method: Per diem plus reimbursable expenses.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Site finding and personal-time services are best compensated by per diem plus reimbursable expenses.
________________________________________
visualReviewNote: Professional Practice subject: no images needed. Text/audio-ready explanation only.

question: Which among the following should the architect’s fee not be based?
keyMemory: Professional fees should be based on scope, service, time, complexity, and project cost, not on the personal profile or status of the client.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Client’s profile directory.
Ulitin natin: the architect’s fee should not be based on the client’s profile directory.
Ito ang pinaka-obvious na wrong basis. Ang professional fee ng architect should be based on professional service, scope, complexity, time, responsibility, personnel involved, or project construction cost. Hindi siya dapat based sa kung sino ang client, gaano siya kayaman, sikat ba siya, or what his social/profile directory says.
Dito may konting nuance: hindi rin normally “contractor’s fee” or “labor cost alone” ang proper basis ng architect’s professional fee. Pero sa choices, ang pinaka-malabo at pinaka-unprofessional basis is client’s profile directory. Kasi walang professional measurement iyon.
Ang correct professional basis sa choices is Project Construction Cost, kasi may percentage fee method based on project construction cost.
Bakit mali ang iba?
a. Contractor’s Fee — hindi ito ideal direct basis. Architect’s fee is separate from contractor’s fee. Pero hindi ito ang pinaka-obvious ethical trap.
c. Labor Cost — too narrow. Labor is only part of construction cost, not the whole basis.
d. Project Construction Cost — ito ang valid basis. Kaya hindi ito ang sagot sa “not based.”
b. Client’s profile directory — tama as the “not based” answer, because fees should not depend on personal status or profile.
Elimination method:
Tanungin mo: “Alin ang professional and measurable basis?” Project Construction Cost.
“Alin ang hindi professional basis at parang discrimination or profiling?” Client profile directory.
Kaya sagot: B.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Professional fees should be based on scope, service, time, complexity, and project cost, not on the personal profile or status of the client.
________________________________________
visualReviewNote: Professional Practice subject: no images needed. Text/audio-ready explanation only.

question: Arch. Casia was commissioned by his friend as the architect for a Townhouse Project. He was specifically asked to prepare brochures for the marketing project. For helping his friend market the project, what type of compensation should Arch. Casia receive?
keyMemory: For marketing or promotional deliverables like brochures, use Professional Fee plus Reimbursable Expenses, not percentage of construction cost.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. Professional Fee + Reimbursable Expenses.
Ulitin natin: Professional Fee plus Reimbursable Expenses.
Bakit? Preparing brochures for marketing is not the usual regular design fee based on construction cost. It is a promotional or pre-design-related service. May professional creative work involved: concept presentation, layout, visuals, coordination, and marketing support. At may actual expenses din: printing, reproduction, materials, transportation, coordination, maybe photography or graphics.
Kaya ang fair method is:
Professional Fee for the architect’s professional service, plus
Reimbursable Expenses for actual out-of-pocket costs.
Bakit mali ang iba?
a. Multiple of Direct Personnel Expenses — useful kung staff-heavy, time-heavy, or office labor-based ang computation. Pero for brochures/marketing support, the clearer answer is professional fee plus reimbursables.
c. Per Diem + Reimbursable Expenses — this is better for personal time, site visits, consultations, or travel-based services. Dito, may specific deliverable: brochures.
d. Percentage of Construction Cost — hindi appropriate kasi brochure preparation is not directly a percentage-of-building-cost service.
Elimination method:
Keyword: brochures for marketing.
May professional service, may production/reproduction costs. Kaya Professional Fee + Reimbursable Expenses.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
For marketing or promotional deliverables like brochures, use Professional Fee plus Reimbursable Expenses, not percentage of construction cost.
________________________________________
visualReviewNote: Professional Practice subject: no images needed. Text/audio-ready explanation only.

question: An Architect drew up plans for a family but then the owners went abroad. After a year, the owners returned but instructed the architect to help them find a new lot. The task would entail the architect’s personal time. How much is the Architect to be compensated after he completes all plans and documents for construction?
keyMemory: Completion of contract documents means the architect has earned about 85% of the basic professional fee.
explanation:
Correct answer: b. 85%.
Ulitin natin: 85%.
Ang clue dito ay completes all plans and documents for construction. Ibig sabihin, natapos na ang Contract Document Phase. Dito ginagawa ang complete working drawings, specifications, schedules, and other documents used for bidding, permit, and construction.
Sa professional fee progress billing, completion of the contract documents is commonly treated as 85% cumulative compensation of the architect’s basic fee.
Bakit hindi 40%? Usually earlier phase pa iyon, around design development type progress.
Bakit hindi 90%? Usually mas malapit ito sa after bidding or negotiation phase.
Bakit hindi 100%? Full 100% is usually after construction phase services or completion of the architect’s full basic service.
Kaya kung plans and documents for construction ang tapos, 85% ang sagot.
Elimination method:
Keyword: all plans and documents for construction.
Working drawings and specs = Contract Document Phase.
Contract Document Phase completion = 85%.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Completion of contract documents means the architect has earned about 85% of the basic professional fee.

visualReviewNote: Professional Practice subject: no images needed. Text/audio-ready explanation only.

question: NOT included in the Project Construction Cost?

choices.text: Architectural interiors designed by the Architect
Site development plan elements designed by the Architect
Alarm and Clock System indicated in the plans prepared by the Architect

keyMemory: Project Construction Cost includes built and installed project items, but excludes the Architect’s professional fee.
explanation:
Correct answer: a. Fees for the Architect.
Ulitin natin: Fees for the Architect are not included in the Project Construction Cost.
Project Construction Cost refers to the cost of constructing the project: materials, labor, equipment, building components, site development items, systems, and other construction-related work indicated in the plans and specifications.
Pero professional fees are different. The architect’s professional fee is not part of the project construction cost. It is compensation for professional services, not construction work.
Bakit mali ang iba?
b. Architectural interiors designed by the Architect — included ito kung part siya ng project scope and construction.
c. Site development plan elements designed by the Architect — included din, kasi site works form part of the construction cost.
d. Alarm and Clock System indicated in the plans — included din, if indicated in the plans and part of the constructed/installed systems.
a. Fees for the Architect — hindi included. This is professional compensation, separate from construction cost.
Elimination method:
Tanungin mo: “Ito ba ay bagay na itatayo, ikakabit, i-install, o gagastusan as construction item?”
Interiors? Yes.
Site development? Yes.
Alarm/clock system? Yes.
Architect’s fee? Hindi construction item. Kaya A.
Ano ang dapat mong tandaan dito?
Project Construction Cost includes built and installed project items, but excludes the Architect’s professional fee.
